items.text: How well-defined is the organization's approach to estimation?

rubric.initial: No defined estimation approach; estimates are ad hoc and based on individual judgment.
rubric.managed: Basic estimation processes exist at project level with some templates available.
rubric.defined: Standardized estimation methodology is defined organization-wide with documented procedures.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Statistical models and historical data systematically inform the estimation approach.
rubric.optimizing: Estimation processes are continuously improved through innovation and predictive model refinement.

items.text: To what extent are estimation methods tailored based on project characteristics?

rubric.initial: One-size-fits-all or no consistent method is applied across projects.
rubric.managed: Some tailoring occurs informally based on project manager experience.
rubric.defined: Documented tailoring guidelines exist for selecting estimation methods by project type.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Tailoring decisions are data-driven using quantitative criteria and past project metrics.
rubric.optimizing: Tailoring guidelines are continuously refined based on outcome analysis and emerging techniques.

items.text: How effectively are estimation tools and techniques utilized?

rubric.initial: No formal tools; estimates rely on spreadsheets or guesswork.
rubric.managed: Basic tools are available but inconsistently used across projects.
rubric.defined: Standard estimation tools are deployed organization-wide with training provided.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Tools integrate with historical databases and provide statistical confidence intervals.
rubric.optimizing: Advanced tools with machine learning capabilities are used and continuously evaluated for effectiveness.

items.text: How effectively does the organization estimate the size of work products?

rubric.initial: Size estimation is not performed or is purely guesswork.
rubric.managed: Size estimates are made using simple methods like expert judgment or analogy.
rubric.defined: Defined sizing methods (function points, story points) are consistently applied with guidelines.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Size estimates are calibrated against historical data with known accuracy ranges.
rubric.optimizing: Size estimation methods are continuously improved using statistical analysis of estimation variance.

items.text: How well are size estimation assumptions documented and validated?

rubric.initial: Assumptions are rarely documented and never validated.
rubric.managed: Key assumptions are occasionally documented but seldom revisited.
rubric.defined: Assumptions are systematically documented and reviewed at milestones.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Assumptions are tracked quantitatively with variance analysis against actuals.
rubric.optimizing: Assumption management is optimized with automated tracking and predictive validation.

items.text: To what degree are decomposition techniques used for size estimation?

rubric.initial: Work is estimated as a whole without decomposition.
rubric.managed: Basic decomposition into major components is sometimes performed.
rubric.defined: Systematic decomposition using defined WBS or feature breakdown structures is standard practice.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Decomposition levels are optimized using statistical analysis of estimation accuracy by granularity.
rubric.optimizing: Decomposition techniques are continuously refined using AI-assisted analysis and cross-project learning.

items.text: How accurately does the organization estimate effort and cost?

rubric.initial: Effort and cost estimates are unreliable with frequent significant overruns.
rubric.managed: Estimates are within reasonable ranges for familiar work but poor for novel efforts.
rubric.defined: Defined models produce estimates that are typically within 20% of actuals.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Parametric models with historical calibration produce estimates within 10% of actuals.
rubric.optimizing: Estimation accuracy is continuously improved and consistently within 5% through advanced modeling.

items.text: How well are effort estimation models calibrated with historical data?

rubric.initial: No historical data is collected or used for estimation.
rubric.managed: Some historical data exists but is inconsistently used to inform estimates.
rubric.defined: Historical data is systematically collected and used to calibrate estimation models.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Statistical regression and parametric models are built from comprehensive historical datasets.
rubric.optimizing: Models self-calibrate using machine learning on continuously updated historical repositories.

items.text: To what extent are cost drivers and risk factors incorporated into estimates?

rubric.initial: Cost drivers are not identified; risk impacts are ignored in estimates.
rubric.managed: Major cost drivers are considered informally; risk contingencies are ad hoc.
rubric.defined: Defined cost driver checklists and risk-adjusted estimation procedures are in place.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Cost drivers are quantitatively modeled with Monte Carlo or similar probabilistic techniques.
rubric.optimizing: Cost driver analysis is continuously refined with real-time market data and predictive analytics.

items.text: How rigorously are estimates reviewed and validated before commitment?

rubric.initial: Estimates are rarely reviewed; commitments are made without validation.
rubric.managed: Informal peer review of estimates occurs on some projects.
rubric.defined: Formal estimation reviews with defined checklists and cross-functional participation are standard.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Estimates are validated using multiple independent methods with statistical comparison.
rubric.optimizing: Validation processes are continuously improved based on post-project accuracy analysis.

items.text: How effectively are estimate-to-actual variances tracked and analyzed?

rubric.initial: No tracking of estimation accuracy occurs.
rubric.managed: Variances are noted at project completion but not systematically analyzed.
rubric.defined: Variance tracking is standard with defined thresholds triggering corrective action.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Statistical process control is applied to estimation variance with trend analysis.
rubric.optimizing: Predictive models anticipate variance patterns and trigger proactive estimate adjustments.

items.text: How well does the organization use estimation variance data to improve future estimates?

rubric.initial: Past estimation errors are not captured or used for improvement.
rubric.managed: Lessons learned occasionally reference estimation issues but lack systematic follow-through.
rubric.defined: Estimation improvement actions are identified from variance analysis and incorporated into methods.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Quantitative estimation improvement targets are set and tracked across the organization.
rubric.optimizing: Continuous estimation improvement is embedded in the culture with automated feedback loops.

items.text: How well does the organization collect and maintain historical estimation data?

rubric.initial: No historical estimation data is collected or maintained.
rubric.managed: Some project data is retained informally by individual project managers.
rubric.defined: A centralized repository of historical estimation data exists with defined collection procedures.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Historical data is curated for quality, normalized across projects, and statistically validated.
rubric.optimizing: Historical data management is optimized with automated collection, cleansing, and cross-referencing.

items.text: How accessible and usable is historical data for estimators?

rubric.initial: Historical data, if it exists, is inaccessible or unusable.
rubric.managed: Some historical data can be found but requires significant effort to locate and interpret.
rubric.defined: Historical data is accessible through a searchable repository with standard formats.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Data is easily queried with filtering by project type, technology, and complexity factors.
rubric.optimizing: Intelligent recommendation systems surface relevant historical comparisons automatically during estimation.

items.text: How effectively is historical data used to benchmark and improve estimation accuracy?

rubric.initial: No benchmarking of estimation performance occurs.
rubric.managed: Occasional comparisons to past projects are made informally.
rubric.defined: Systematic benchmarking against historical baselines is part of the estimation process.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Statistical benchmarking with confidence intervals drives calibration of estimation models.
rubric.optimizing: Continuous benchmarking with industry data and cross-organizational comparisons drives best-in-class accuracy.

items.text: How comprehensive and structured are project work plans?

rubric.initial: No formal work plans exist; work is assigned reactively.
rubric.managed: Basic project plans cover major milestones and deliverables.
rubric.defined: Comprehensive work plans with WBS, dependencies, and defined work packages are standard.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Plans are quantitatively modeled with critical path analysis and resource optimization.
rubric.optimizing: Planning processes are continuously improved using predictive analytics and automated optimization.

items.text: How effectively are work breakdown structures developed and maintained?

rubric.initial: WBS is not used or is created inconsistently.
rubric.managed: Basic WBS exists for major projects but lacks detail and consistency.
rubric.defined: Standardized WBS templates and development procedures are defined and consistently applied.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: WBS quality is measured and optimized using decomposition metrics and historical patterns.
rubric.optimizing: WBS development is continuously enhanced with automated decomposition and pattern recognition.

items.text: How well are planning assumptions and constraints documented?

rubric.initial: Assumptions and constraints are rarely identified or documented.
rubric.managed: Major assumptions are listed but not systematically tracked.
rubric.defined: All planning assumptions and constraints are documented, categorized, and reviewed regularly.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Assumptions are quantitatively risk-assessed and tracked with automated constraint monitoring.
rubric.optimizing: Assumption management is optimized with predictive detection of assumption violations.

items.text: How effectively are resources identified, allocated, and managed?

rubric.initial: Resource allocation is ad hoc with frequent conflicts and shortages.
rubric.managed: Basic resource planning identifies key roles but lacks capacity management.
rubric.defined: Defined resource planning processes include skills matching, capacity analysis, and allocation procedures.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Resource utilization is quantitatively tracked and optimized across the portfolio.
rubric.optimizing: Resource planning uses predictive models to anticipate needs and optimize allocation dynamically.

items.text: How well does the organization manage skill requirements and gaps?

rubric.initial: Skill requirements are not assessed; gaps are discovered reactively.
rubric.managed: Basic skill inventories exist but are not systematically matched to project needs.
rubric.defined: Skill gap analysis is performed for each project with defined mitigation strategies.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Skill supply-demand is modeled quantitatively across the organization with trend analysis.
rubric.optimizing: Skill management is optimized with predictive workforce planning and proactive development programs.

items.text: How effectively is resource capacity managed across multiple projects?

rubric.initial: No cross-project resource visibility exists; conflicts are common.
rubric.managed: Resource conflicts are resolved reactively through escalation.
rubric.defined: Enterprise resource management provides visibility and defined allocation processes.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Capacity is quantitatively modeled with what-if analysis for portfolio-level optimization.
rubric.optimizing: Dynamic resource optimization uses real-time data and predictive algorithms.

items.text: How rigorously are project schedules developed with realistic timelines?

rubric.initial: Schedules are arbitrary dates driven by external pressure without analysis.
rubric.managed: Schedules are based on basic task lists with estimated durations.
rubric.defined: Schedules use network analysis with dependencies, float calculations, and resource leveling.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Schedules incorporate probabilistic analysis with confidence intervals for key milestones.
rubric.optimizing: Scheduling processes are continuously refined using schedule performance analytics and predictive modeling.

items.text: How effectively are schedule dependencies and critical paths managed?

rubric.initial: Dependencies are not identified; critical path is unknown.
rubric.managed: Major dependencies are identified but not formally managed.
rubric.defined: Critical path analysis is standard with dependency tracking and impact assessment.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Dependencies are quantitatively risk-assessed with schedule simulation and sensitivity analysis.
rubric.optimizing: Dependency management is optimized with real-time tracking and automated critical path updates.

items.text: How well are schedule buffers and contingencies incorporated?

rubric.initial: No buffers exist; schedules have zero margin.
rubric.managed: Informal padding is added to individual tasks without a systematic approach.
rubric.defined: Buffer management follows defined guidelines with project and feeding buffers.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Buffers are statistically sized based on historical variance and risk analysis.
rubric.optimizing: Buffer management is continuously optimized using consumption rate analysis and trend prediction.

items.text: How effectively are stakeholder commitments obtained and documented?

rubric.initial: Commitments are informal and poorly documented; expectations are unclear.
rubric.managed: Key stakeholder commitments are documented in project charters or kickoff meetings.
rubric.defined: Formal commitment processes include documented agreements, sign-offs, and responsibility matrices.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Commitment compliance is tracked quantitatively with automated escalation for deviations.
rubric.optimizing: Commitment management is continuously improved with stakeholder satisfaction analytics.

items.text: How well are commitment conflicts identified and resolved?

rubric.initial: Commitment conflicts go undetected until they cause project failures.
rubric.managed: Conflicts are identified reactively and resolved through escalation.
rubric.defined: Proactive conflict identification is part of planning with defined resolution procedures.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Conflict patterns are analyzed quantitatively to predict and prevent recurring issues.
rubric.optimizing: Conflict resolution is optimized with automated detection and proactive stakeholder alignment.

items.text: How effectively are stakeholder expectations managed throughout the project lifecycle?

rubric.initial: Stakeholder expectations are not managed; surprises are common.
rubric.managed: Periodic status updates keep key stakeholders informed of major changes.
rubric.defined: Defined communication plans ensure stakeholders receive appropriate information at each lifecycle stage.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Stakeholder satisfaction is quantitatively measured and used to adjust communication strategies.
rubric.optimizing: Stakeholder expectation management is continuously refined using feedback analytics and sentiment analysis.

items.text: How effectively are plans updated to reflect actual progress and changes?

rubric.initial: Plans are created once and never updated; they quickly become irrelevant.
rubric.managed: Plans are updated occasionally for major changes but not maintained regularly.
rubric.defined: Regular plan updates are scheduled with defined triggers for replanning.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Plan maintenance is integrated with earned value analysis and automated variance detection.
rubric.optimizing: Plans are living documents with real-time updates driven by automated data feeds and predictive adjustments.

items.text: How well does the organization manage the relationship between plans and actual performance?

rubric.initial: No comparison between planned and actual performance is made.
rubric.managed: Occasional comparisons highlight major deviations but lack systematic analysis.
rubric.defined: Regular plan-vs-actual reviews with defined corrective action procedures are standard.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Statistical analysis of plan-vs-actual trends drives systematic planning improvements.
rubric.optimizing: Predictive analytics anticipate deviations before they occur, enabling proactive plan adjustments.

items.text: How effectively are planning lessons learned captured and applied to future projects?

rubric.initial: No lessons learned are captured from planning activities.
rubric.managed: Informal lessons are occasionally discussed but rarely documented or applied.
rubric.defined: Formal lessons learned reviews capture planning improvements with defined application procedures.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Quantitative analysis of planning effectiveness drives systematic improvements to planning methods.
rubric.optimizing: Continuous planning improvement is embedded in the culture with automated best practice dissemination.

items.text: How effectively does the organization monitor project performance against plans?

rubric.initial: No systematic monitoring occurs; issues are discovered late.
rubric.managed: Basic status tracking covers schedule and budget at a high level.
rubric.defined: Defined monitoring processes use earned value, milestone tracking, and quality metrics.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Statistical process control and trend analysis provide early warning of performance issues.
rubric.optimizing: Predictive performance monitoring uses AI-driven analytics to forecast outcomes and trigger proactive responses.

items.text: How well are key performance indicators defined and tracked?

rubric.initial: No KPIs are defined for projects.
rubric.managed: Basic KPIs exist but are inconsistently tracked and reported.
rubric.defined: Standardized KPI sets are defined by project type with automated collection and dashboards.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: KPIs are statistically validated for predictive value and continuously calibrated.
rubric.optimizing: KPI frameworks are continuously optimized using outcome correlation analysis and industry benchmarking.

items.text: How effectively are performance thresholds and triggers defined for corrective action?

rubric.initial: No thresholds exist; problems are addressed only when they become critical.
rubric.managed: Informal thresholds exist for major metrics like budget and schedule.
rubric.defined: Defined threshold frameworks with escalation procedures and response timelines are in place.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Thresholds are statistically derived from historical performance distributions.
rubric.optimizing: Threshold management is continuously optimized with adaptive triggers based on project context and risk profile.

items.text: How effectively are corrective actions identified and implemented when issues arise?

rubric.initial: Problems persist without structured corrective action; firefighting is the norm.
rubric.managed: Corrective actions are taken for major issues but lack systematic tracking.
rubric.defined: Defined corrective action processes include root cause analysis, action plans, and follow-up verification.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Corrective action effectiveness is quantitatively measured with trend analysis across projects.
rubric.optimizing: Corrective action processes are continuously improved using predictive issue detection and proactive prevention.

items.text: How well are corrective actions tracked to closure?

rubric.initial: Corrective actions are rarely tracked; many are abandoned before completion.
rubric.managed: Major corrective actions are tracked informally through project status meetings.
rubric.defined: All corrective actions are logged in a tracking system with owners, deadlines, and verification criteria.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Closure rates and time-to-resolution are statistically analyzed to identify systemic bottlenecks.
rubric.optimizing: Automated tracking with intelligent escalation and predictive closure timing drives optimal resolution.

items.text: How effectively does the organization prevent recurrence of identified issues?

rubric.initial: Issues recur frequently; no systematic prevention exists.
rubric.managed: Major issues sometimes result in process changes, but prevention is inconsistent.
rubric.defined: Root cause analysis drives preventive actions that are incorporated into standard processes.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Recurrence rates are quantitatively tracked and used to validate preventive action effectiveness.
rubric.optimizing: Predictive analytics identify potential issues before they occur, driving proactive prevention strategies.

items.text: How effectively are project milestones defined and tracked?

rubric.initial: Milestones are vague or nonexistent.
rubric.managed: Major milestones are defined but criteria for completion are unclear.
rubric.defined: Milestones have defined entry/exit criteria, deliverables, and formal review gates.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Milestone achievement probability is quantitatively forecasted using schedule performance data.
rubric.optimizing: Milestone management is continuously optimized with predictive tracking and automated gate reviews.

items.text: How well does milestone tracking integrate with overall project governance?

rubric.initial: No integration between milestone tracking and governance exists.
rubric.managed: Milestone status is reported to governance bodies but lacks structured decision-making.
rubric.defined: Milestone reviews are integrated into governance with defined decision criteria and escalation paths.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Governance decisions are informed by quantitative milestone trend analysis and risk assessments.
rubric.optimizing: Integrated governance uses real-time milestone analytics and predictive models for proactive decision-making.

items.text: How effectively are milestone dependencies across projects managed?

rubric.initial: Cross-project milestone dependencies are not identified.
rubric.managed: Major cross-project dependencies are known but not formally managed.
rubric.defined: Cross-project milestone dependencies are mapped, tracked, and managed through portfolio coordination.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Dependency impacts are quantitatively modeled with cascade analysis across the portfolio.
rubric.optimizing: Automated dependency management with predictive impact analysis enables proactive cross-project coordination.

items.text: How effectively is project data collected, stored, and maintained?

rubric.initial: Project data is scattered across individual files with no standard approach.
rubric.managed: Basic project data is collected but storage is inconsistent and retrieval is difficult.
rubric.defined: Standardized data collection procedures with centralized repositories and defined data quality standards exist.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Data quality is quantitatively measured and managed with automated validation and cleansing.
rubric.optimizing: Data management is continuously optimized with intelligent curation and automated cross-referencing.

items.text: How well does the organization ensure data integrity and accessibility?

rubric.initial: Data integrity is not managed; data is frequently inaccurate or outdated.
rubric.managed: Basic data validation exists but integrity issues are common.
rubric.defined: Defined data governance ensures integrity through validation rules, access controls, and audit trails.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Data integrity metrics are tracked with statistical quality control applied to key datasets.
rubric.optimizing: Automated data integrity assurance with real-time anomaly detection and self-correcting mechanisms.

items.text: How effectively is data used to support project decision-making?

rubric.initial: Decisions are made without reference to project data.
rubric.managed: Data is consulted informally but analysis is rudimentary.
rubric.defined: Data-driven decision-making is standard with defined analysis procedures and decision frameworks.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Advanced analytics and visualization tools enable quantitative decision support.
rubric.optimizing: Predictive analytics and AI-driven insights enable proactive, optimized decision-making.

items.text: How effectively are progress reports produced and communicated to stakeholders?

rubric.initial: Progress reporting is absent or inconsistent.
rubric.managed: Basic status reports are produced periodically but vary in format and content.
rubric.defined: Standardized progress reports with defined content, frequency, and distribution are in place.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Reports include quantitative performance analysis with trend visualization and forecasting.
rubric.optimizing: Automated, real-time reporting with personalized stakeholder dashboards and predictive insights.

items.text: How well do progress reports reflect the true status of projects?

rubric.initial: Reports are often misleading or omit critical issues.
rubric.managed: Reports generally reflect status but may lack objectivity or completeness.
rubric.defined: Reports are verified against objective data with defined accuracy standards and review processes.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Report accuracy is quantitatively validated through independent data verification.
rubric.optimizing: Report integrity is assured through automated data feeds with anomaly detection and bias correction.

items.text: How effectively does reporting drive timely management action?

rubric.initial: Reports are produced but rarely drive action.
rubric.managed: Major issues in reports sometimes trigger management response.
rubric.defined: Reports include action items with owners and deadlines, tracked through governance processes.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Reporting effectiveness is measured by correlation between report insights and management decisions.
rubric.optimizing: Intelligent reporting prioritizes critical insights and triggers automated escalation workflows.

items.text: How effectively are stakeholder needs and expectations elicited?

rubric.initial: Requirements are assumed or dictated without stakeholder input.
rubric.managed: Basic stakeholder interviews are conducted for major requirements.
rubric.defined: Defined elicitation techniques (workshops, prototyping, surveys) are applied systematically.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Elicitation effectiveness is quantitatively measured and optimized by technique and context.
rubric.optimizing: Elicitation processes are continuously improved using stakeholder satisfaction analytics and AI-assisted need discovery.

items.text: How well are diverse stakeholder perspectives captured and reconciled?

rubric.initial: Only the most vocal stakeholders' needs are captured; conflicts go unresolved.
rubric.managed: Multiple stakeholders are consulted but conflict resolution is informal.
rubric.defined: Stakeholder analysis identifies all relevant parties with defined conflict resolution procedures.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Stakeholder coverage metrics ensure comprehensive representation with quantitative conflict analysis.
rubric.optimizing: Automated stakeholder sentiment analysis and predictive conflict detection optimize comprehensive elicitation.

items.text: How effectively are implicit and emergent requirements identified?

rubric.initial: Only explicitly stated requirements are captured; implicit needs are missed.
rubric.managed: Experienced team members sometimes identify unstated requirements informally.
rubric.defined: Defined techniques for uncovering implicit requirements (domain analysis, user observation) are standard.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Pattern analysis of past projects quantitatively identifies commonly missed requirement categories.
rubric.optimizing: Machine learning on historical requirement patterns proactively surfaces likely implicit requirements.

items.text: How rigorously are requirements analyzed for completeness, consistency, and feasibility?

rubric.initial: Requirements are accepted without analysis; inconsistencies are discovered during development.
rubric.managed: Basic reviews check for obvious gaps and contradictions in major requirements.
rubric.defined: Formal analysis processes verify completeness, consistency, feasibility, and testability with defined criteria.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Analysis quality is quantitatively measured with defect detection effectiveness metrics.
rubric.optimizing: Analysis processes are continuously optimized using AI-assisted consistency checking and gap detection.

items.text: How well are requirements prioritized based on business value and risk?

rubric.initial: No prioritization exists; all requirements are treated equally.
rubric.managed: Informal prioritization by stakeholder influence rather than objective criteria.
rubric.defined: Defined prioritization frameworks (MoSCoW, weighted scoring) are consistently applied.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Prioritization models incorporate quantitative value, cost, and risk analysis.
rubric.optimizing: Dynamic prioritization continuously adjusts based on real-time market data and value realization metrics.

items.text: How effectively are requirement dependencies and impacts analyzed?

rubric.initial: Dependencies between requirements are not analyzed.
rubric.managed: Major dependencies are identified informally during planning.
rubric.defined: Systematic dependency analysis with impact matrices and cross-reference documentation is standard.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Dependency networks are quantitatively modeled with cascade impact analysis.
rubric.optimizing: Automated dependency detection and impact prediction optimize requirement sequencing and risk management.

items.text: How well are requirements documented with clear, measurable acceptance criteria?

rubric.initial: Requirements are vague statements without measurable criteria.
rubric.managed: Key requirements have basic acceptance criteria but many lack specificity.
rubric.defined: All requirements follow defined templates with measurable acceptance criteria and quality attributes.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Requirements quality is quantitatively measured (ambiguity indices, testability scores) and tracked.
rubric.optimizing: Requirements specification is continuously improved through automated quality analysis and best practice evolution.

items.text: How effectively are non-functional requirements specified?

rubric.initial: Non-functional requirements are ignored or stated vaguely.
rubric.managed: Major non-functional requirements (performance, security) are mentioned but not quantified.
rubric.defined: Non-functional requirements are systematically identified and specified with measurable targets.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Non-functional requirement coverage is quantitatively tracked against a comprehensive taxonomy.
rubric.optimizing: Non-functional requirement specification is optimized using industry benchmarks and automated gap analysis.

items.text: How well are requirements baselined and approved?

rubric.initial: No formal baselining occurs; requirements drift continuously.
rubric.managed: Major requirements are approved but baselining is informal.
rubric.defined: Formal baseline procedures with sign-off, version control, and change management are standard.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Baseline quality and stability are quantitatively measured with change rate metrics.
rubric.optimizing: Baseline management is optimized with predictive stability analysis and automated approval workflows.

items.text: How well does the organization maintain bidirectional traceability of requirements?

rubric.initial: No traceability exists between requirements and other work products.
rubric.managed: Basic forward traceability exists for critical requirements.
rubric.defined: Bidirectional traceability from stakeholder needs through requirements to design, code, and tests is maintained.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Traceability coverage and quality metrics are tracked with automated consistency checking.
rubric.optimizing: Traceability management is continuously optimized with intelligent link suggestion and gap detection.

items.text: How effectively is traceability used to assess change impact?

rubric.initial: Change impacts are assessed without reference to traceability information.
rubric.managed: Traceability is occasionally consulted for major changes.
rubric.defined: Traceability is systematically used for impact analysis on all requirement changes.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Quantitative impact models leverage traceability data for effort and risk estimation.
rubric.optimizing: Automated impact analysis using traceability graphs provides instant, comprehensive change assessment.

items.text: How well is traceability maintained as the system evolves?

rubric.initial: Traceability links, if they exist, become stale immediately.
rubric.managed: Traceability is updated for major releases but gaps accumulate.
rubric.defined: Defined procedures ensure traceability is updated with every requirement or design change.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Traceability freshness metrics are tracked with automated staleness detection.
rubric.optimizing: Self-maintaining traceability uses automated detection of related changes to keep links current.

items.text: How effectively are requirement changes controlled and managed?

rubric.initial: Changes are made without control; scope creep is rampant.
rubric.managed: A change request process exists for major changes but is often bypassed.
rubric.defined: Formal change control with impact analysis, approval boards, and documented decisions is standard.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Change patterns are quantitatively analyzed to identify systemic causes and predict future change volumes.
rubric.optimizing: Change management is continuously optimized with predictive change modeling and proactive scope management.

items.text: How well does the change control process balance agility with governance?

rubric.initial: Either no governance exists or the process is so rigid it prevents necessary changes.
rubric.managed: Basic governance exists but frequently creates bottlenecks for urgent changes.
rubric.defined: Tiered change control provides appropriate governance levels based on change impact and urgency.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Process efficiency metrics drive optimization of governance levels and approval cycle times.
rubric.optimizing: Adaptive governance automatically adjusts control levels based on project phase, risk, and change characteristics.

items.text: How effectively are change decisions communicated to all affected parties?

rubric.initial: Change decisions are poorly communicated; affected parties are often unaware.
rubric.managed: Key stakeholders are notified of major changes through status updates.
rubric.defined: Automated change notifications reach all traced stakeholders with defined communication procedures.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Communication effectiveness is measured and optimized using acknowledgment and comprehension metrics.
rubric.optimizing: Intelligent change communication tailors content and channel to each stakeholder's role and impact level.

items.text: How well-defined is the organization's approach to technical design?

rubric.initial: No defined design approach; solutions are developed ad hoc.
rubric.managed: Basic design practices exist but vary by team and project.
rubric.defined: Standardized design processes with templates, reviews, and quality criteria are defined organization-wide.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Design quality is quantitatively measured with defect density and rework metrics by design phase.
rubric.optimizing: Design approaches are continuously improved using design pattern analytics and emerging technology evaluation.

items.text: How effectively are design alternatives evaluated?

rubric.initial: A single design approach is assumed without exploring alternatives.
rubric.managed: Alternatives are informally discussed but evaluation is subjective.
rubric.defined: Formal alternative analysis with defined evaluation criteria and trade-off documentation is standard.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Alternative evaluation uses quantitative models with weighted scoring and sensitivity analysis.
rubric.optimizing: Design alternative evaluation is continuously enhanced with simulation, modeling, and automated scoring.

items.text: How well does the design approach address quality attributes and constraints?

rubric.initial: Quality attributes are not considered during design.
rubric.managed: Major quality attributes are considered informally.
rubric.defined: Quality attribute requirements systematically drive architectural decisions with documented trade-offs.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Quality attribute achievement is quantitatively predicted through modeling and verified through testing.
rubric.optimizing: Quality-driven design is continuously optimized using pattern libraries and automated quality prediction.

items.text: How effectively are components designed with appropriate modularity and cohesion?

rubric.initial: Components are monolithic with tight coupling and low cohesion.
rubric.managed: Some modularity exists but component boundaries are poorly defined.
rubric.defined: Components are designed following defined modularity guidelines with documented interfaces.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Component quality metrics (coupling, cohesion, complexity) are quantitatively tracked and managed.
rubric.optimizing: Component design is continuously optimized using automated quality analysis and architectural fitness functions.

items.text: How well are component specifications documented?

rubric.initial: Component specifications are absent; knowledge exists only in developers' heads.
rubric.managed: Basic specifications exist for major components but lack completeness.
rubric.defined: Comprehensive component specifications with interfaces, behaviors, and constraints are standard.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Specification quality and completeness metrics are tracked with automated verification.
rubric.optimizing: Specifications are continuously improved with automated generation from models and self-updating documentation.

items.text: How effectively is design complexity managed?

rubric.initial: Complexity is not managed; technical debt accumulates unchecked.
rubric.managed: Major complexity issues are addressed reactively when they cause problems.
rubric.defined: Complexity management guidelines with defined thresholds and refactoring procedures are in place.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Complexity metrics are quantitatively tracked with trend analysis and early warning systems.
rubric.optimizing: Proactive complexity management uses predictive models to prevent complexity growth before it occurs.

items.text: How effectively does the organization evaluate build, buy, or reuse options?

rubric.initial: All solutions are custom-built without considering alternatives.
rubric.managed: Buy vs. build is discussed informally for major components.
rubric.defined: Formal evaluation frameworks compare build, buy, and reuse options with defined criteria.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Decision models incorporate total cost of ownership, risk, and strategic alignment with quantitative analysis.
rubric.optimizing: Build/buy/reuse analysis is continuously optimized using outcome tracking and market intelligence.

items.text: How well does the organization manage and promote reuse of assets?

rubric.initial: No reuse strategy exists; components are rebuilt for each project.
rubric.managed: Some reuse occurs informally when developers are aware of existing components.
rubric.defined: A reuse library with cataloging, quality standards, and search capabilities is maintained.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Reuse rates and ROI are quantitatively tracked and used to drive reuse strategy improvements.
rubric.optimizing: Reuse is optimized with intelligent asset recommendation, automated compatibility analysis, and proactive asset development.

items.text: How effectively are commercial and open-source components evaluated and integrated?

rubric.initial: Third-party components are selected without evaluation criteria.
rubric.managed: Basic evaluation considers functionality and cost for major procurements.
rubric.defined: Defined evaluation criteria cover functionality, quality, support, licensing, security, and long-term viability.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Component selection effectiveness is quantitatively tracked with vendor performance and integration cost metrics.
rubric.optimizing: Component evaluation is continuously improved with automated market scanning and predictive lifecycle analysis.

items.text: How well-defined and enforced are coding and implementation standards?

rubric.initial: No standards exist; implementation practices vary widely.
rubric.managed: Basic coding guidelines exist but enforcement is inconsistent.
rubric.defined: Comprehensive standards are defined with automated enforcement through linters and code analysis tools.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Standards compliance is quantitatively measured with continuous monitoring and trend analysis.
rubric.optimizing: Standards are continuously evolved based on defect correlation analysis and industry best practice adoption.

items.text: How effectively is technical debt identified and managed?

rubric.initial: Technical debt is not tracked; it accumulates without visibility.
rubric.managed: Major technical debt items are known but not systematically managed.
rubric.defined: Technical debt is inventoried, prioritized, and addressed through defined reduction plans.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Technical debt is quantitatively measured with impact analysis on velocity, quality, and maintenance costs.
rubric.optimizing: Technical debt management is optimized with automated detection, impact prediction, and strategic reduction planning.

items.text: How well does the organization ensure implementation quality through reviews and inspections?

rubric.initial: No code reviews or inspections are performed.
rubric.managed: Informal code reviews occur for critical components.
rubric.defined: Mandatory code reviews with defined checklists and quality gates are standard practice.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Review effectiveness is quantitatively measured by defect detection rates and review efficiency metrics.
rubric.optimizing: Review processes are continuously optimized with AI-assisted code analysis and intelligent reviewer assignment.

items.text: How effectively are internal and external interfaces designed and documented?

rubric.initial: Interfaces are undocumented; integration issues are common.
rubric.managed: Major interfaces are documented but specifications are often incomplete or outdated.
rubric.defined: Interface specifications follow defined standards with versioning and compatibility management.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Interface quality metrics (stability, compatibility, performance) are quantitatively tracked.
rubric.optimizing: Interface design is continuously optimized with automated compatibility checking and evolution analysis.

items.text: How well are interface contracts managed across component and system boundaries?

rubric.initial: No interface contracts exist; integration is trial and error.
rubric.managed: Basic agreements exist between major component teams.
rubric.defined: Formal interface control documents with defined change management processes are maintained.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Interface contract compliance is quantitatively monitored with automated verification.
rubric.optimizing: Interface management is optimized with contract-driven development and automated compatibility testing.

items.text: How effectively does the organization manage interface evolution and backward compatibility?

rubric.initial: Interface changes break consumers without notice.
rubric.managed: Major interface changes are communicated but backward compatibility is not guaranteed.
rubric.defined: Interface versioning and deprecation policies ensure managed evolution with transition periods.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Interface stability and migration cost metrics drive quantitative management of evolution strategies.
rubric.optimizing: Predictive interface evolution planning uses consumer analysis and automated migration tooling.

items.text: How well-defined is the organization's integration strategy?

rubric.initial: No integration strategy exists; components are assembled in an ad hoc manner.
rubric.managed: Basic integration plans identify the order of component assembly.
rubric.defined: A defined integration strategy specifies approach, sequence, environments, and criteria.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Integration strategies are quantitatively evaluated using historical integration defect and effort data.
rubric.optimizing: Integration strategies are continuously optimized through automated analysis of integration outcomes and patterns.

items.text: How effectively are integration environments established and managed?

rubric.initial: No dedicated integration environments exist; integration occurs in development environments.
rubric.managed: Basic integration environments are available but not consistently managed.
rubric.defined: Dedicated integration environments mirror production with defined configuration management.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Environment quality and availability metrics are tracked with automated provisioning and monitoring.
rubric.optimizing: Environment management is optimized with infrastructure-as-code, self-healing, and predictive capacity management.

items.text: How well does integration planning account for risk and complexity?

rubric.initial: Integration risks are not considered; complex integrations fail unexpectedly.
rubric.managed: Major integration risks are informally identified.
rubric.defined: Risk-based integration planning adjusts sequence and testing depth based on documented risk analysis.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Integration risk is quantitatively modeled using component complexity and interface stability metrics.
rubric.optimizing: Predictive risk models dynamically adjust integration strategies based on real-time quality signals.

items.text: How effectively are component interfaces verified during integration?

rubric.initial: Interface compatibility is not verified until system testing.
rubric.managed: Basic interface testing occurs for major integration points.
rubric.defined: Systematic interface verification with defined test cases and compatibility matrices is standard.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Interface verification coverage and defect data are quantitatively analyzed to optimize test strategies.
rubric.optimizing: Automated continuous interface verification with intelligent test selection and real-time compatibility monitoring.

items.text: How well are interface issues identified, tracked, and resolved during integration?

rubric.initial: Interface issues are discovered late and resolved through workarounds.
rubric.managed: Interface issues are logged but tracking and resolution are informal.
rubric.defined: Formal interface issue management with root cause analysis and corrective action procedures is standard.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Interface issue patterns are quantitatively analyzed to drive preventive interface design improvements.
rubric.optimizing: Predictive interface issue detection enables proactive resolution before integration begins.

items.text: How effectively are interface agreements enforced across teams and suppliers?

rubric.initial: No interface agreements exist between integration partners.
rubric.managed: Informal agreements exist but enforcement is inconsistent.
rubric.defined: Formal interface control documents are maintained with defined compliance verification procedures.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Agreement compliance is quantitatively measured with automated verification and reporting.
rubric.optimizing: Interface agreement management is optimized with real-time compliance monitoring and automated enforcement.

items.text: How well-defined are the procedures for assembling components into the product?

rubric.initial: Assembly is manual and undocumented; results are inconsistent.
rubric.managed: Basic assembly instructions exist for major subsystems.
rubric.defined: Comprehensive assembly procedures with checklists, verification steps, and rollback plans are defined.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Assembly success rates and efficiency are quantitatively tracked to optimize procedures.
rubric.optimizing: Assembly is fully automated with continuous integration pipelines and self-healing build processes.

items.text: How effectively is assembly automation implemented?

rubric.initial: All assembly is manual with no automation.
rubric.managed: Some assembly steps are automated for major builds.
rubric.defined: Continuous integration with automated build, packaging, and basic verification is standard.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Build pipeline effectiveness is quantitatively measured with cycle time, success rate, and defect escape metrics.
rubric.optimizing: Build automation is continuously optimized with intelligent pipeline orchestration and predictive failure detection.

items.text: How well is the assembly process documented and repeatable?

rubric.initial: Assembly depends on individual knowledge; it cannot be reliably repeated.
rubric.managed: Basic documentation exists but key steps rely on tribal knowledge.
rubric.defined: Assembly processes are fully documented, version-controlled, and executable by any trained team member.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Process repeatability is quantitatively verified through regular independent execution and variance measurement.
rubric.optimizing: Assembly documentation is self-updating and continuously verified through automated execution and comparison.

items.text: How comprehensive is integration testing?

rubric.initial: Integration testing is minimal or nonexistent.
rubric.managed: Key integration paths are tested manually.
rubric.defined: Comprehensive integration test suites cover all interfaces with defined coverage criteria.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Integration test effectiveness is quantitatively measured by defect detection rates and production escape analysis.
rubric.optimizing: Integration testing is continuously optimized with risk-based test selection and AI-generated test scenarios.

items.text: How effectively are integration test results used to assess readiness for delivery?

rubric.initial: Test results do not influence delivery decisions.
rubric.managed: Major integration failures delay delivery but no formal readiness criteria exist.
rubric.defined: Defined integration test pass criteria and quality gates determine delivery readiness.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Readiness assessments use quantitative quality models combining multiple test metrics and trend analysis.
rubric.optimizing: Predictive readiness models provide confidence-based delivery recommendations using historical outcome data.

items.text: How well is integration testing automated?

rubric.initial: All integration testing is manual.
rubric.managed: Some integration tests are automated for critical paths.
rubric.defined: Comprehensive automated integration test suites run as part of the CI/CD pipeline.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Automation coverage and effectiveness metrics drive continuous improvement of the test suite.
rubric.optimizing: Self-maintaining test suites with AI-generated tests and intelligent failure analysis optimize coverage.

items.text: How effectively are integrated products delivered to customers or stakeholders?

rubric.initial: Delivery is ad hoc with frequent issues during deployment.
rubric.managed: Basic delivery procedures exist but are inconsistently followed.
rubric.defined: Defined delivery processes include packaging, release notes, installation procedures, and verification.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Delivery success metrics are quantitatively tracked with root cause analysis of delivery issues.
rubric.optimizing: Delivery is continuously optimized with automated deployment, canary releases, and instant rollback capabilities.

items.text: How well are delivery environments and configurations managed?

rubric.initial: No delivery environment management; deployments fail due to environment differences.
rubric.managed: Basic environment documentation exists but configuration drift is common.
rubric.defined: Environment configurations are managed as code with automated provisioning and drift detection.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Environment consistency metrics are quantitatively tracked with automated compliance verification.
rubric.optimizing: Environment management is optimized with self-provisioning, predictive capacity, and zero-downtime deployment.

items.text: How effectively are delivery issues identified and resolved?

rubric.initial: Delivery issues cause extended outages with no structured response.
rubric.managed: Major delivery issues are addressed reactively with basic rollback procedures.
rubric.defined: Defined delivery issue management includes automated monitoring, alerting, and incident response procedures.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Delivery issue patterns are quantitatively analyzed to drive preventive improvements.
rubric.optimizing: Predictive deployment monitoring with automated rollback and self-healing eliminates most delivery issues.

items.text: How well-defined is the organization's approach to verification?

rubric.initial: No defined verification approach; testing is ad hoc and inconsistent.
rubric.managed: Basic testing occurs but verification methods are not standardized.
rubric.defined: Defined verification strategy specifies methods, coverage criteria, and responsibilities for each lifecycle phase.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Verification effectiveness is quantitatively measured with defect detection efficiency and phase containment metrics.
rubric.optimizing: Verification approaches are continuously optimized using defect escape analysis and method effectiveness studies.

items.text: How effectively are verification activities planned and resourced?

rubric.initial: Verification is an afterthought with inadequate time and resources.
rubric.managed: Basic test planning occurs but verification is often compressed when schedules slip.
rubric.defined: Verification planning is integrated into project planning with defined effort estimates and resource allocation.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Verification resource needs are quantitatively modeled based on product complexity and quality targets.
rubric.optimizing: Verification planning is continuously optimized using predictive models and dynamic resource allocation.

items.text: How well does verification cover functional and non-functional requirements?

rubric.initial: Verification focuses only on basic functional testing; non-functional aspects are ignored.
rubric.managed: Key non-functional requirements are tested informally.
rubric.defined: Comprehensive verification plans cover functional, performance, security, usability, and other quality attributes.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Coverage metrics by requirement type are tracked with gap analysis driving test strategy improvements.
rubric.optimizing: Verification coverage is continuously optimized with risk-based prioritization and automated gap detection.

items.text: How effectively are peer reviews conducted on work products?

rubric.initial: Peer reviews are not performed.
rubric.managed: Informal reviews occur for some deliverables based on individual initiative.
rubric.defined: Formal peer review processes with defined roles, checklists, and criteria are standard for all key work products.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Review effectiveness is quantitatively measured with defect detection rates and review efficiency metrics.
rubric.optimizing: Peer review processes are continuously improved using defect prediction models and intelligent reviewer matching.

items.text: How well do peer reviews cover diverse work product types?

rubric.initial: Only code is reviewed, if anything.
rubric.managed: Code and major design documents are sometimes reviewed.
rubric.defined: All work product types (requirements, designs, code, tests, documentation) have defined review procedures.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Review coverage by work product type is tracked with defect density analysis driving coverage decisions.
rubric.optimizing: Comprehensive review coverage is optimized using product risk profiles and automated review support tools.

items.text: How effectively are peer review findings tracked and resolved?

rubric.initial: Review findings are discussed but not tracked or followed up.
rubric.managed: Major findings are logged but closure tracking is informal.
rubric.defined: All findings are tracked in a system with resolution verification and metrics reporting.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Finding resolution patterns are quantitatively analyzed to improve review preparation and focus areas.
rubric.optimizing: Intelligent finding management predicts likely issues and optimizes review focus areas proactively.

items.text: How effectively does the organization validate that products meet stakeholder needs?

rubric.initial: No systematic validation occurs; stakeholder satisfaction is unknown until delivery.
rubric.managed: Basic user acceptance testing is performed near the end of the project.
rubric.defined: Defined validation processes engage stakeholders throughout the lifecycle with progressive validation activities.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Validation effectiveness is quantitatively measured by post-delivery defect rates and stakeholder satisfaction.
rubric.optimizing: Validation is continuously optimized with early stakeholder feedback loops and predictive satisfaction modeling.

items.text: How well are validation environments representative of the operational environment?

rubric.initial: Validation occurs in development environments that differ significantly from production.
rubric.managed: Basic representative environments exist but with notable gaps.
rubric.defined: Validation environments closely replicate the operational environment with defined fidelity criteria.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Environment fidelity is quantitatively measured and managed with automated configuration comparison.
rubric.optimizing: Environment management ensures near-perfect fidelity with automated synchronization and predictive drift detection.

items.text: How effectively are validation results used to drive product improvements?

rubric.initial: Validation results are not analyzed for improvement opportunities.
rubric.managed: Major validation failures lead to fixes but no systematic improvement.
rubric.defined: Validation results are systematically analyzed to identify improvement opportunities in both product and process.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Quantitative analysis of validation findings drives targeted improvements with measurable effectiveness tracking.
rubric.optimizing: Continuous improvement loops from validation use pattern analysis and predictive models to prevent issues.

items.text: How well are acceptance criteria defined and agreed upon with stakeholders?

rubric.initial: Acceptance criteria are vague or nonexistent.
rubric.managed: Basic acceptance criteria exist for major features but lack measurability.
rubric.defined: All requirements have defined, measurable acceptance criteria agreed upon with stakeholders.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Acceptance criteria quality is quantitatively assessed using testability and completeness metrics.
rubric.optimizing: Acceptance criteria development is continuously improved using outcome correlation and stakeholder feedback analysis.

items.text: How effectively are acceptance criteria used to drive testing?

rubric.initial: Tests are designed without reference to acceptance criteria.
rubric.managed: Some tests map to acceptance criteria but coverage is incomplete.
rubric.defined: Acceptance criteria directly drive test case design with full traceability and coverage verification.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Acceptance test coverage and effectiveness are quantitatively tracked with gap analysis.
rubric.optimizing: Acceptance testing is continuously optimized with automated test generation from criteria and risk-based prioritization.

items.text: How well are acceptance decisions documented and communicated?

rubric.initial: Acceptance decisions are informal and poorly documented.
rubric.managed: Major acceptance decisions are documented but rationale is limited.
rubric.defined: All acceptance decisions are formally documented with rationale, conditions, and stakeholder sign-off.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Decision quality and timeliness metrics are tracked with trend analysis.
rubric.optimizing: Acceptance decision processes are optimized with automated evidence compilation and decision support dashboards.

items.text: How effectively is test coverage measured and managed?

rubric.initial: Test coverage is not measured; testing is based on time available.
rubric.managed: Basic coverage metrics (e.g., code coverage) are collected for some projects.
rubric.defined: Comprehensive coverage metrics including requirements, code, and risk coverage are tracked and managed.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Coverage effectiveness is quantitatively correlated with production defect rates to optimize coverage targets.
rubric.optimizing: Coverage management is continuously improved with risk-adaptive coverage targets and intelligent gap identification.

items.text: How well is test automation implemented to support coverage goals?

rubric.initial: No test automation exists.
rubric.managed: Some unit tests and basic automated checks exist for critical components.
rubric.defined: Comprehensive test automation supports unit, integration, system, and regression testing with defined coverage targets.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Automation ROI and defect detection effectiveness are quantitatively tracked to optimize investment.
rubric.optimizing: Test automation is continuously evolved with AI-generated tests, self-healing locators, and predictive test selection.

items.text: How effectively are coverage gaps identified and addressed?

rubric.initial: Coverage gaps are unknown.
rubric.managed: Major coverage gaps are identified during testing but not systematically addressed.
rubric.defined: Gap analysis processes identify uncovered areas with defined procedures for gap closure.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Gap patterns are quantitatively analyzed across projects to drive systemic coverage improvements.
rubric.optimizing: Automated coverage gap detection with risk-based prioritization and intelligent test generation closes gaps proactively.

items.text: How effectively does the organization select suppliers?

rubric.initial: Suppliers are selected based on personal relationships or lowest cost without evaluation.
rubric.managed: Basic evaluation considers cost and capability for major procurements.
rubric.defined: Defined supplier selection processes use weighted criteria covering capability, quality, cost, and risk.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Supplier selection effectiveness is quantitatively tracked with outcome analysis informing criteria refinement.
rubric.optimizing: Supplier selection is continuously optimized using predictive supplier performance models and market intelligence.

items.text: How well does the organization assess supplier capability and capacity?

rubric.initial: Supplier capability is assumed without assessment.
rubric.managed: Basic capability checks are performed for critical suppliers.
rubric.defined: Comprehensive supplier assessments evaluate technical capability, capacity, quality systems, and financial stability.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Supplier assessment accuracy is quantitatively validated against actual performance outcomes.
rubric.optimizing: Predictive supplier capability models continuously improve assessment criteria and risk identification.

items.text: How effectively are supplier risks evaluated during selection?

rubric.initial: Supplier risks are not considered during selection.
rubric.managed: Major supplier risks are informally discussed.
rubric.defined: Systematic risk assessment is part of the selection process with documented mitigation strategies.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Supplier risk models are quantitatively validated using historical supplier performance data.
rubric.optimizing: Predictive risk analytics proactively identify emerging supplier risks and inform selection decisions.

items.text: How well are supplier agreements structured with clear terms and deliverables?

rubric.initial: Agreements are informal or use generic terms without project-specific requirements.
rubric.managed: Basic contracts exist but often lack clear deliverables, quality criteria, or acceptance procedures.
rubric.defined: Comprehensive agreements define deliverables, quality standards, acceptance criteria, IP rights, and dispute resolution.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Agreement effectiveness is quantitatively analyzed with clause-level compliance tracking.
rubric.optimizing: Agreement templates are continuously improved using compliance analytics and industry best practices.

items.text: How effectively do agreements address quality, schedule, and risk requirements?

rubric.initial: Agreements focus only on cost; quality and schedule terms are vague.
rubric.managed: Basic quality and schedule requirements are included but lack measurable criteria.
rubric.defined: Agreements include specific, measurable quality, schedule, and risk management requirements with penalties and incentives.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Agreement term effectiveness is quantitatively correlated with supplier performance outcomes.
rubric.optimizing: Agreement structures are continuously optimized using outcome analytics and behavioral incentive modeling.

items.text: How well are intellectual property and security requirements addressed in agreements?

rubric.initial: IP and security are not addressed in supplier agreements.
rubric.managed: Basic IP and NDA clauses exist but are not tailored to specific engagements.
rubric.defined: Comprehensive IP, security, and data protection terms are defined with compliance verification procedures.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: IP and security compliance is quantitatively monitored with automated verification and reporting.
rubric.optimizing: IP and security management is optimized with predictive risk assessment and adaptive contractual protections.

items.text: How effectively is supplier performance monitored against agreement terms?

rubric.initial: Supplier performance is not monitored until delivery failure occurs.
rubric.managed: Basic performance tracking occurs for critical suppliers at major milestones.
rubric.defined: Systematic performance monitoring uses defined KPIs, regular reviews, and documented scorecards.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Supplier performance is quantitatively benchmarked with trend analysis and predictive forecasting.
rubric.optimizing: Real-time supplier performance monitoring with predictive analytics enables proactive intervention.

items.text: How well are supplier issues escalated and resolved?

rubric.initial: Supplier issues fester without escalation or structured resolution.
rubric.managed: Major supplier issues are escalated but resolution processes are informal.
rubric.defined: Defined escalation procedures with tiered response levels and documented resolution tracking are in place.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Issue resolution effectiveness is quantitatively measured with root cause analysis driving systemic improvements.
rubric.optimizing: Predictive issue detection enables proactive supplier engagement before issues impact delivery.

items.text: How effectively are supplier relationships managed for long-term value?

rubric.initial: Supplier relationships are transactional with no long-term perspective.
rubric.managed: Key suppliers have ongoing relationships but strategic management is limited.
rubric.defined: Strategic supplier relationship management includes regular reviews, joint improvement initiatives, and partnership development.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Relationship value is quantitatively measured with ROI analysis and strategic alignment metrics.
rubric.optimizing: Supplier ecosystem management is optimized with collaborative innovation programs and value co-creation.

items.text: How effectively are ongoing supplier risks identified and managed?

rubric.initial: Supplier risks are ignored until they materialize as problems.
rubric.managed: Major supplier risks are known but not systematically managed.
rubric.defined: Supplier risk registers are maintained with defined monitoring and mitigation procedures.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Supplier risk is quantitatively modeled with portfolio-level exposure analysis and correlation tracking.
rubric.optimizing: Predictive supplier risk management uses market intelligence and early warning systems for proactive mitigation.

items.text: How well are supplier continuity and contingency plans maintained?

rubric.initial: No contingency plans exist for supplier disruption.
rubric.managed: Basic contingency awareness exists for critical suppliers.
rubric.defined: Documented contingency plans with alternative suppliers and transition procedures are maintained and tested.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Contingency plan effectiveness is quantitatively validated through simulation exercises with measured outcomes.
rubric.optimizing: Contingency management is continuously optimized with automated market scanning and pre-qualified alternatives.

items.text: How effectively does the organization manage concentration risk across suppliers?

rubric.initial: Concentration risk is not assessed; single points of failure exist.
rubric.managed: Major concentration risks are known but not actively managed.
rubric.defined: Supplier diversification strategies are defined with concentration limits and monitoring procedures.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Concentration risk is quantitatively modeled with scenario analysis and portfolio optimization.
rubric.optimizing: Dynamic supplier portfolio management continuously optimizes risk-reward balance across the supply base.

items.text: How effectively are supplier transitions planned and executed?

rubric.initial: Supplier transitions are chaotic with significant knowledge loss and service disruption.
rubric.managed: Basic transition plans are created for major supplier changes.
rubric.defined: Comprehensive transition plans with knowledge transfer, parallel operation, and acceptance criteria are standard.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Transition effectiveness is quantitatively measured with defined success criteria and lessons learned.
rubric.optimizing: Transition management is continuously optimized with reusable frameworks and predictive risk assessment.

items.text: How well is knowledge transfer managed during supplier transitions?

rubric.initial: Knowledge transfer is not planned; critical knowledge is lost during transitions.
rubric.managed: Key knowledge areas are identified for transfer but execution is inconsistent.
rubric.defined: Structured knowledge transfer processes with defined deliverables, verification, and retention procedures are in place.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Knowledge transfer completeness is quantitatively measured with validated retention testing.
rubric.optimizing: Knowledge transfer is optimized with automated knowledge capture and intelligent transfer planning.

items.text: How effectively are transition risks mitigated?

rubric.initial: Transition risks are not assessed; disruptions are common.
rubric.managed: Major transition risks are informally identified.
rubric.defined: Transition risk assessments with defined mitigation strategies and contingency plans are standard.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Transition risk models are quantitatively validated using historical transition outcome data.
rubric.optimizing: Predictive transition risk models enable proactive risk mitigation with automated contingency activation.

items.text: How systematically does the organization identify risks?

rubric.initial: Risks are not identified proactively; they are recognized only when they materialize.
rubric.managed: Key risks are identified informally at project start but not revisited.
rubric.defined: Systematic risk identification using checklists, brainstorming, and analysis techniques is performed throughout the lifecycle.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Risk identification effectiveness is quantitatively measured by comparing identified risks to materialized issues.
rubric.optimizing: Risk identification is continuously enhanced with AI-driven pattern recognition and cross-project risk intelligence.

items.text: How well are risk categories and taxonomies used to ensure comprehensive identification?

rubric.initial: No risk categories exist; identification is unsystematic.
rubric.managed: Basic categories (technical, schedule, cost) guide informal risk discussions.
rubric.defined: Comprehensive risk taxonomies tailored to the organization ensure consistent coverage across all risk dimensions.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Taxonomy effectiveness is quantitatively validated by analyzing risk category coverage against actual outcomes.
rubric.optimizing: Risk taxonomies are continuously evolved using outcome data, industry trends, and emerging risk pattern analysis.

items.text: How effectively are risks identified across all organizational levels and projects?

rubric.initial: Risk identification occurs only at the project level, if at all.
rubric.managed: Some enterprise risks are recognized but cross-project risk identification is limited.
rubric.defined: Multi-level risk identification covers project, program, and enterprise levels with aggregation procedures.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Cross-level risk correlation is quantitatively analyzed to identify systemic risk patterns.
rubric.optimizing: Integrated risk intelligence provides real-time risk visibility across all levels with automated escalation.

items.text: How rigorously are risks analyzed for probability and impact?

rubric.initial: Risk analysis is not performed; risks are simply listed without assessment.
rubric.managed: Basic qualitative assessment (high/medium/low) is applied to major risks.
rubric.defined: Structured risk analysis using defined probability and impact scales with scoring matrices is standard.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Quantitative risk analysis uses Monte Carlo simulation, decision trees, and sensitivity analysis.
rubric.optimizing: Risk analysis is continuously refined with advanced statistical models and real-time data integration.

items.text: How effectively are risk interactions and cascading effects analyzed?

rubric.initial: Risk interactions are not considered; each risk is analyzed in isolation.
rubric.managed: Major risk dependencies are informally recognized.
rubric.defined: Risk interaction analysis identifies dependencies and potential cascading effects systematically.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Risk network models quantitatively assess correlation and cascade probabilities.
rubric.optimizing: Dynamic risk network analysis with real-time correlation monitoring enables predictive cascade prevention.

items.text: How well are risk analysis results communicated to decision-makers?

rubric.initial: Risk information rarely reaches decision-makers.
rubric.managed: Major risks are reported to management periodically.
rubric.defined: Risk analysis results are regularly communicated through defined reporting with actionable recommendations.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Risk dashboards provide quantitative risk exposure analysis with trend visualization for decision-makers.
rubric.optimizing: Intelligent risk communication tailors content and urgency to each stakeholder's decision context.

items.text: How effectively are risk mitigation strategies developed and implemented?

rubric.initial: No mitigation strategies are developed; risks are accepted passively.
rubric.managed: Basic mitigation actions are identified for critical risks.
rubric.defined: Comprehensive mitigation strategies (avoid, transfer, mitigate, accept) are developed with action plans and owners.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Mitigation effectiveness is quantitatively measured with cost-benefit analysis driving strategy optimization.
rubric.optimizing: Mitigation strategies are continuously improved using outcome analysis and predictive effectiveness models.

items.text: How well are mitigation resources and budgets allocated?

rubric.initial: No resources are allocated for risk mitigation.
rubric.managed: Ad hoc resources are sometimes available for critical risk mitigation.
rubric.defined: Risk mitigation budgets and resources are planned and allocated as part of project planning.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Mitigation resource allocation is optimized using quantitative risk-return analysis.
rubric.optimizing: Dynamic resource allocation uses real-time risk exposure data to optimize mitigation investment.

items.text: How effectively are contingency plans developed for high-impact risks?

rubric.initial: No contingency plans exist.
rubric.managed: Informal contingency ideas exist for the most obvious risks.
rubric.defined: Documented contingency plans with triggers, procedures, and required resources are maintained for high-impact risks.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Contingency plan effectiveness is quantitatively validated through exercises and simulations.
rubric.optimizing: Contingency management is continuously improved with automated trigger monitoring and rapid activation procedures.

items.text: How effectively does the organization identify and capture opportunities?

rubric.initial: Opportunities are not systematically identified; focus is only on threats.
rubric.managed: Opportunities are occasionally recognized but rarely pursued proactively.
rubric.defined: Systematic opportunity identification is integrated into risk management with defined evaluation and capture processes.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Opportunity capture rates and value realization are quantitatively tracked and analyzed.
