rubric.optimizing: Opportunity management is continuously enhanced with market intelligence and innovation scanning capabilities.

items.text: How well does the organization evaluate and prioritize opportunities?

rubric.initial: Opportunities are not evaluated; response is reactive.
rubric.managed: Promising opportunities are informally discussed.
rubric.defined: Structured opportunity evaluation with defined criteria, value analysis, and prioritization is standard.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Opportunity portfolios are quantitatively managed with ROI modeling and strategic alignment scoring.
rubric.optimizing: Dynamic opportunity evaluation uses real-time market data and predictive value modeling for optimal prioritization.

items.text: How effectively are captured opportunities integrated into project and organizational plans?

rubric.initial: Opportunities are not incorporated into plans.
rubric.managed: Major opportunities sometimes influence project direction informally.
rubric.defined: Opportunity action plans are integrated into project plans with defined resource allocation and success metrics.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Opportunity integration effectiveness is quantitatively measured with value realization tracking.
rubric.optimizing: Seamless opportunity integration uses automated planning updates and resource optimization algorithms.

items.text: How effectively are risks monitored throughout the project lifecycle?

rubric.initial: Risks are identified at project start and never revisited.
rubric.managed: Risk registers are reviewed periodically at major milestones.
rubric.defined: Continuous risk monitoring with defined review cycles, trigger tracking, and status updates is standard.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Risk trends are quantitatively analyzed with statistical early warning indicators.
rubric.optimizing: Real-time risk monitoring with predictive analytics and automated escalation enables proactive risk management.

items.text: How well are risk indicators and early warning systems established?

rubric.initial: No risk indicators or early warning systems exist.
rubric.managed: Informal indicators are watched by experienced project managers.
rubric.defined: Defined risk indicators with monitoring procedures and threshold-based alerting are in place.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Indicator effectiveness is quantitatively validated by correlation with risk materialization events.
rubric.optimizing: Self-calibrating risk indicators use machine learning to continuously improve detection accuracy.

items.text: How effectively are risk management outcomes used to improve organizational risk practices?

rubric.initial: No learning from risk management outcomes occurs.
rubric.managed: Major risk events sometimes generate lessons learned.
rubric.defined: Systematic post-project risk reviews capture lessons that are incorporated into organizational risk practices.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Quantitative analysis of risk management effectiveness drives systemic improvements to methods and tools.
rubric.optimizing: Continuous risk practice improvement uses automated outcome analysis and industry benchmarking.

items.text: How well does the organization define criteria for significant decisions?

rubric.initial: Decisions are made without defined criteria; they are based on intuition or authority.
rubric.managed: Basic criteria are discussed informally for major decisions.
rubric.defined: Defined decision criteria frameworks ensure consistent, transparent evaluation for all significant decisions.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Criteria effectiveness is quantitatively validated by correlating criteria scores with decision outcomes.
rubric.optimizing: Decision criteria are continuously refined using outcome analytics and behavioral decision science insights.

items.text: How effectively are decision criteria aligned with organizational objectives?

rubric.initial: No alignment between decision criteria and organizational objectives.
rubric.managed: Major decisions consider organizational goals informally.
rubric.defined: Decision criteria explicitly map to organizational objectives with defined weighting and trade-off procedures.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Criteria-objective alignment is quantitatively measured and optimized across the decision portfolio.
rubric.optimizing: Dynamic criteria alignment uses real-time strategic data to ensure decisions reflect current organizational priorities.

items.text: How well are decision-making thresholds defined for when formal analysis is required?

rubric.initial: No thresholds exist; formal analysis is never or always applied regardless of decision significance.
rubric.managed: Informal judgment determines when formal analysis is needed.
rubric.defined: Defined thresholds based on cost, risk, and strategic impact determine when formal decision analysis is required.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Threshold effectiveness is quantitatively validated by analyzing outcomes of formally and informally made decisions.
rubric.optimizing: Adaptive thresholds automatically adjust based on organizational risk appetite and decision outcome analysis.

items.text: How effectively are alternatives generated and evaluated for significant decisions?

rubric.initial: Alternatives are not generated; the first option is accepted.
rubric.managed: A few obvious alternatives are considered informally.
rubric.defined: Systematic alternative generation and evaluation using defined methods ensures comprehensive consideration.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Alternative evaluation effectiveness is quantitatively measured with outcome tracking against predictions.
rubric.optimizing: Alternative evaluation is continuously enhanced with creative ideation techniques and predictive outcome modeling.

items.text: How well are evaluation results documented and communicated?

rubric.initial: Decision rationale is not documented.
rubric.managed: Major decisions have informal documentation of the chosen option.
rubric.defined: Comprehensive decision packages document alternatives, evaluation results, rationale, and stakeholder input.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Documentation quality and completeness are quantitatively assessed with usage tracking.
rubric.optimizing: Intelligent decision documentation is automated with template generation and rationale extraction.

items.text: How effectively does the organization prevent bias in alternative evaluation?

rubric.initial: Decision bias is not recognized or addressed.
rubric.managed: Awareness of common biases exists but no mitigation measures are in place.
rubric.defined: Defined bias mitigation techniques (devil's advocate, independent evaluation, blind scoring) are used.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Bias prevalence is quantitatively measured through decision audit analysis.
rubric.optimizing: Continuous bias reduction uses behavioral analytics and AI-assisted evaluation to minimize cognitive biases.

items.text: How effectively are trade studies conducted for technical and business decisions?

rubric.initial: Trade studies are not performed.
rubric.managed: Informal pros-and-cons analysis is used for major decisions.
rubric.defined: Structured trade studies with defined methodology, criteria weighting, and sensitivity analysis are standard.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Trade study accuracy is quantitatively validated by comparing predictions to actual outcomes.
rubric.optimizing: Trade study methods are continuously enhanced with simulation modeling and multi-objective optimization.

items.text: How well are trade study assumptions and uncertainties managed?

rubric.initial: Assumptions are not identified; uncertainties are ignored.
rubric.managed: Major assumptions are noted but not validated or tracked.
rubric.defined: Assumptions are documented with confidence levels and uncertainty ranges incorporated into the analysis.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Assumption sensitivity is quantitatively analyzed to identify which assumptions most affect the decision.
rubric.optimizing: Dynamic assumption management tracks validation status and automatically triggers re-analysis when assumptions change.

items.text: How effectively are trade study results integrated into project decisions?

rubric.initial: Trade study results, if they exist, are ignored in final decisions.
rubric.managed: Trade study results influence major decisions informally.
rubric.defined: Trade study results are formally integrated into decision-making with documented rationale for any deviations.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Integration effectiveness is quantitatively measured by tracking decision-outcome alignment with trade study recommendations.
rubric.optimizing: Seamless integration of trade study results into decision workflows with automated impact tracking.

items.text: How well are decisions documented for future reference and accountability?

rubric.initial: Decisions are not documented; rationale is lost over time.
rubric.managed: Major decisions are noted in meeting minutes with limited context.
rubric.defined: Formal decision records capture the problem, alternatives, criteria, analysis, decision, and rationale.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Decision record quality and completeness are quantitatively assessed with automated compliance checking.
rubric.optimizing: Decision documentation is continuously improved with intelligent templates and automated knowledge extraction.

items.text: How effectively is decision history used to improve future decisions?

rubric.initial: No decision history is maintained or referenced.
rubric.managed: Past decisions are occasionally referenced informally.
rubric.defined: Decision history is maintained in a searchable repository and referenced during similar future decisions.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Decision outcome analysis quantitatively identifies patterns and biases to improve future decision quality.
rubric.optimizing: Intelligent decision support uses historical outcome analysis and pattern matching to recommend optimal approaches.

items.text: How well is decision provenance maintained for audit and compliance purposes?

rubric.initial: No audit trail exists for decisions.
rubric.managed: Basic records exist but lack completeness for audit purposes.
rubric.defined: Complete decision audit trails with sign-offs, timestamps, and supporting documentation are maintained.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Audit trail completeness is quantitatively verified with automated compliance checking.
rubric.optimizing: Decision provenance management is continuously optimized with blockchain-grade integrity and automated compliance reporting.

items.text: How effectively are decisions escalated when they exceed defined authority levels?

rubric.initial: No escalation procedures exist; decisions are made without appropriate authority.
rubric.managed: Major decisions are escalated informally based on individual judgment.
rubric.defined: Defined escalation procedures with authority levels, criteria, and timelines ensure appropriate decision authority.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Escalation effectiveness is quantitatively measured by decision cycle time and authority-level appropriateness.
rubric.optimizing: Escalation processes are continuously optimized with intelligent routing and predictive authority matching.

items.text: How well does the escalation process preserve decision context and urgency?

rubric.initial: Context is lost during escalation; decision-makers lack necessary information.
rubric.managed: Basic context is provided during escalation but often incomplete.
rubric.defined: Escalation packages include comprehensive context, analysis, recommendations, and urgency classification.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Escalation context quality is quantitatively measured by decision-maker satisfaction and decision cycle time.
rubric.optimizing: Intelligent escalation packaging automatically compiles relevant context and routes to optimal decision-makers.

items.text: How effectively are escalated decisions communicated back to affected parties?

rubric.initial: Escalated decisions are poorly communicated; affected parties remain uninformed.
rubric.managed: Major escalated decisions are communicated through management channels.
rubric.defined: Defined communication procedures ensure all affected parties are promptly informed of escalated decisions.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Communication effectiveness is quantitatively measured by acknowledgment rates and action compliance.
rubric.optimizing: Automated escalation resolution communication with targeted notifications and impact-specific messaging.

items.text: How effectively are configuration items identified and cataloged?

rubric.initial: Configuration items are not identified; no one knows what needs to be controlled.
rubric.managed: Major deliverables are informally recognized as configuration items.
rubric.defined: Systematic CI identification uses defined criteria with a comprehensive CI catalog maintained.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: CI identification coverage is quantitatively measured against project artifacts with gap analysis.
rubric.optimizing: CI identification is continuously optimized with automated discovery and intelligent classification.

items.text: How well are configuration item attributes and relationships documented?

rubric.initial: No CI attributes or relationships are documented.
rubric.managed: Basic attributes (name, version) are recorded for major items.
rubric.defined: Comprehensive CI attributes and relationships are documented in a configuration management database.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: CMDB quality metrics are tracked with automated consistency and completeness verification.
rubric.optimizing: CMDB management is optimized with automated relationship discovery and self-updating attribute maintenance.

items.text: How effectively is the scope of configuration management determined for each project?

rubric.initial: CM scope is not defined; it varies arbitrarily.
rubric.managed: CM scope informally covers major deliverables.
rubric.defined: CM scope is defined for each project based on documented criteria and tailoring guidelines.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: CM scope effectiveness is quantitatively validated against change-related defect and rework data.
rubric.optimizing: CM scope optimization uses predictive models to balance control overhead against risk reduction.

items.text: How effectively are changes to configuration items controlled?

rubric.initial: Changes are made without any control; anyone can modify anything.
rubric.managed: Basic version control is used for source code but not for other work products.
rubric.defined: Comprehensive change control processes govern all CIs with defined approval workflows and impact analysis.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Change control efficiency is quantitatively measured with cycle time, approval accuracy, and error rate metrics.
rubric.optimizing: Change control is continuously optimized with intelligent impact analysis and automated approval routing.

items.text: How well does the change control board process function?

rubric.initial: No change control board exists.
rubric.managed: An informal review group handles major changes when escalated.
rubric.defined: A defined CCB with clear membership, authority, procedures, and decision criteria operates regularly.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: CCB effectiveness is quantitatively measured by decision quality, cycle time, and stakeholder satisfaction.
rubric.optimizing: CCB processes are continuously improved with data-driven meeting optimization and predictive change assessment.

items.text: How effectively are emergency changes handled while maintaining control?

rubric.initial: Emergency changes bypass all controls; retroactive tracking rarely occurs.
rubric.managed: Emergency changes follow abbreviated procedures but are inconsistently documented.
rubric.defined: Defined emergency change procedures provide rapid response while maintaining audit trails and post-hoc review.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Emergency change frequency and impact are quantitatively tracked with trend analysis driving preventive improvements.
rubric.optimizing: Emergency change management is optimized with pre-authorized change templates and automated post-implementation verification.

items.text: How effectively is the status of configuration items tracked and reported?

rubric.initial: No status tracking of configuration items exists.
rubric.managed: Basic version information is maintained for major items.
rubric.defined: Comprehensive status accounting tracks all CI states, changes, and approvals with regular reporting.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Status accounting quality is quantitatively verified with automated consistency checking.
rubric.optimizing: Status accounting is optimized with real-time tracking and intelligent anomaly detection.

items.text: How well are configuration status reports used for decision-making?

rubric.initial: Configuration status information is not available for decisions.
rubric.managed: Basic version information is occasionally referenced.
rubric.defined: Status reports are regularly produced and used for release, change, and quality decisions.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Report utility is quantitatively measured by correlation with decision quality and cycle time.
rubric.optimizing: Intelligent status reporting proactively surfaces decision-relevant information with actionable insights.

items.text: How effectively is configuration history maintained for traceability?

rubric.initial: No configuration history is maintained.
rubric.managed: Basic change logs exist for source code.
rubric.defined: Complete configuration history with change rationale, approvals, and temporal context is maintained for all CIs.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: History completeness and accuracy are quantitatively verified with automated audit procedures.
rubric.optimizing: Configuration history management is optimized with automated capture and intelligent temporal querying.

items.text: How effectively are configuration audits performed?

rubric.initial: Configuration audits are not performed.
rubric.managed: Informal checks occur before major releases.
rubric.defined: Formal functional and physical configuration audits are performed at defined milestones.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Audit effectiveness is quantitatively measured by finding rates and post-release configuration issues.
rubric.optimizing: Configuration audits are continuously improved with automated verification and risk-based audit scoping.

items.text: How well are audit findings tracked and resolved?

rubric.initial: Audit findings are not tracked.
rubric.managed: Major findings are noted but resolution is informal.
rubric.defined: All audit findings are formally tracked with owners, deadlines, and verification of resolution.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Finding patterns are quantitatively analyzed to identify systemic CM process improvements.
rubric.optimizing: Predictive audit analytics focus audits on highest-risk areas and automate finding resolution verification.

items.text: How effectively do audits verify consistency between baselines and actual configurations?

rubric.initial: No baseline verification is performed.
rubric.managed: Basic checks compare delivered items against release manifests.
rubric.defined: Systematic verification ensures complete consistency between baselines and actual configurations.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Consistency metrics are quantitatively tracked with automated drift detection and alerting.
rubric.optimizing: Continuous baseline verification with automated remediation ensures perpetual consistency.

items.text: How effectively are baselines established and maintained throughout the lifecycle?

rubric.initial: No baselines are established; there is no known-good reference point.
rubric.managed: Baselines are established for major releases but not maintained between releases.
rubric.defined: Baselines are established at defined points with documented contents, approval, and controlled evolution.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Baseline quality and stability metrics are tracked with quantitative management of baseline changes.
rubric.optimizing: Baseline management is continuously optimized with automated establishment and intelligent evolution control.

items.text: How well are baseline contents defined and controlled?

rubric.initial: Baseline contents are undefined; it is unclear what constitutes a baseline.
rubric.managed: Major components are included in baselines but completeness varies.
rubric.defined: Baseline content definitions are comprehensive with automated assembly and verification procedures.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Baseline completeness is quantitatively verified with automated content validation.
rubric.optimizing: Intelligent baseline management automatically assembles, validates, and promotes baselines based on quality criteria.

items.text: How effectively does the organization manage multiple concurrent baselines?

rubric.initial: Only one version exists at a time; parallel baselines are not managed.
rubric.managed: Multiple versions are maintained informally with frequent confusion.
rubric.defined: Branching and merging strategies with defined procedures manage multiple concurrent baselines.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Branch health metrics are quantitatively tracked with merge complexity and conflict rate analysis.
rubric.optimizing: Concurrent baseline management is optimized with automated merge conflict prevention and intelligent branch strategies.

items.text: How effectively does the organization perform root cause analysis?

rubric.initial: Root cause analysis is not performed; symptoms are addressed without understanding underlying causes.
rubric.managed: Basic root cause analysis is performed for major incidents using informal techniques.
rubric.defined: Structured RCA using defined techniques (5 Whys, fishbone, fault tree) is standard for all significant defects.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: RCA effectiveness is quantitatively measured by recurrence rates and time-to-resolution improvements.
rubric.optimizing: RCA practices are continuously enhanced with advanced analytical techniques and AI-assisted pattern recognition.

items.text: How well are root cause analysis results documented and shared?

rubric.initial: RCA results are not documented.
rubric.managed: Major RCA findings are documented but not widely shared.
rubric.defined: All RCA results are documented in a searchable knowledge base with defined sharing and notification procedures.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: RCA documentation quality and usage are quantitatively tracked with effectiveness metrics.
rubric.optimizing: RCA knowledge management is optimized with intelligent categorization and proactive relevance-based distribution.

items.text: How effectively are root causes categorized and trended across the organization?

rubric.initial: No categorization or trending of root causes occurs.
rubric.managed: Major root cause categories are informally recognized.
rubric.defined: Root causes are systematically categorized using defined taxonomies with periodic trend reporting.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Trend analysis uses statistical methods to identify significant patterns and systemic root causes.
rubric.optimizing: Predictive root cause analytics anticipate emerging defect patterns before they materialize.

items.text: How effectively does the organization prevent defect recurrence?

rubric.initial: Defects recur frequently; no prevention measures are in place.
rubric.managed: Major recurring defects sometimes lead to process changes.
rubric.defined: Systematic defect prevention processes translate RCA findings into preventive process and product changes.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Prevention effectiveness is quantitatively measured by defect density trends and recurrence rates.
rubric.optimizing: Defect prevention is continuously optimized with predictive defect modeling and proactive process enhancement.

items.text: How well are preventive actions prioritized and implemented across the organization?

rubric.initial: Preventive actions are not identified or implemented.
rubric.managed: Some preventive actions are implemented locally without organizational coordination.
rubric.defined: Preventive actions are prioritized by impact and feasibility and deployed across affected organizational units.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Preventive action ROI is quantitatively tracked with cost-benefit analysis driving prioritization.
rubric.optimizing: Preventive action management is optimized with automated impact assessment and organization-wide deployment tracking.

items.text: How effectively are lessons from defect prevention shared across projects?

rubric.initial: No cross-project sharing of defect prevention lessons occurs.
rubric.managed: Major lessons are occasionally shared through informal channels.
rubric.defined: Defined knowledge sharing mechanisms ensure defect prevention lessons reach all relevant projects and teams.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Knowledge sharing effectiveness is quantitatively measured by adoption rates and downstream defect reduction.
rubric.optimizing: Intelligent knowledge dissemination automatically routes relevant prevention lessons to teams based on context.

items.text: How effectively are improvement actions derived from causal analysis?

rubric.initial: No improvement actions result from defect analysis.
rubric.managed: Obvious fixes are implemented for major defects.
rubric.defined: Structured improvement action planning translates causal analysis into specific, measurable process improvements.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Improvement action effectiveness is quantitatively tracked with pre/post metrics and trend analysis.
rubric.optimizing: Improvement action planning is continuously refined using effectiveness prediction models and outcome optimization.

items.text: How well are improvement actions tracked to completion and verified?

rubric.initial: Improvement actions are identified but rarely completed.
rubric.managed: Major improvement actions are tracked informally.
rubric.defined: All improvement actions are tracked in a system with owners, deadlines, and verification criteria.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Action completion rates and verification results are quantitatively analyzed with bottleneck identification.
rubric.optimizing: Automated improvement action management ensures completion with intelligent follow-up and impact verification.

items.text: How effectively is the impact of improvement actions measured?

rubric.initial: Improvement action impact is not measured.
rubric.managed: Anecdotal evidence is used to assess major improvements.
rubric.defined: Defined metrics measure the impact of improvement actions with before/after comparison.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Statistical significance testing validates improvement action impact with confidence intervals.
rubric.optimizing: Continuous impact measurement with automated attribution analysis optimizes improvement investment decisions.

items.text: How effectively does the organization analyze defect and issue trends?

rubric.initial: No trend analysis of defects or issues occurs.
rubric.managed: Basic defect counts are tracked but not analyzed for trends.
rubric.defined: Systematic trend analysis identifies patterns in defect types, origins, and lifecycle phases.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Statistical trend analysis with control charts and prediction models is applied to defect data.
rubric.optimizing: Advanced trend analytics with machine learning identify emerging patterns and predict future defect profiles.

items.text: How well are trends used to focus improvement efforts?

rubric.initial: Trends do not inform improvement priorities.
rubric.managed: Major trends informally influence where attention is directed.
rubric.defined: Trend analysis directly drives improvement project selection and resource allocation.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Quantitative trend impact analysis prioritizes improvements by predicted defect reduction and cost savings.
rubric.optimizing: Dynamic trend-driven improvement planning continuously adjusts priorities based on real-time trend data.

items.text: How effectively are trends communicated to stakeholders and decision-makers?

rubric.initial: Trend information is not shared with stakeholders.
rubric.managed: Major trends are occasionally presented to management.
rubric.defined: Regular trend reports are distributed to relevant stakeholders with actionable recommendations.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Trend dashboards provide real-time quantitative insights with drill-down capability for all stakeholders.
rubric.optimizing: Intelligent trend communication tailors content and recommendations to each stakeholder's decision context.

items.text: How effectively is adherence to defined processes evaluated?

rubric.initial: Process adherence is not evaluated; processes are defined but compliance is unknown.
rubric.managed: Basic compliance checks occur for major projects but are not systematic.
rubric.defined: Systematic process adherence evaluations are conducted using defined criteria, checklists, and schedules.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Adherence rates are quantitatively tracked with trend analysis driving targeted process improvements.
rubric.optimizing: Process adherence management is continuously optimized with automated compliance monitoring and predictive risk assessment.

items.text: How well are process adherence evaluations independent and objective?

rubric.initial: No independence in quality evaluation; teams self-assess without external review.
rubric.managed: Some external review occurs but independence is not guaranteed.
rubric.defined: Independent PQA function conducts objective evaluations with defined authority and reporting lines.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: PQA independence and objectivity are quantitatively measured with finding validity analysis.
rubric.optimizing: PQA effectiveness is continuously optimized through independent benchmarking and method evolution.

items.text: How effectively are process tailoring decisions evaluated for appropriateness?

rubric.initial: Process tailoring is uncontrolled; compliance cannot be assessed against unknown baselines.
rubric.managed: Major tailoring decisions are reviewed informally.
rubric.defined: Tailoring decisions are evaluated against defined guidelines with documented rationale and approval.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Tailoring effectiveness is quantitatively correlated with project outcomes to refine tailoring guidelines.
rubric.optimizing: Tailoring evaluation is optimized with predictive models that recommend optimal tailoring based on project characteristics.

items.text: How effectively are work products evaluated against quality criteria?

rubric.initial: Work products are not evaluated against quality criteria.
rubric.managed: Major deliverables undergo basic quality checks.
rubric.defined: Systematic product evaluations using defined quality criteria and checklists are performed at key milestones.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Product quality is quantitatively measured with statistical analysis of defect density and quality attribute achievement.
rubric.optimizing: Product evaluation is continuously improved with automated quality analysis and predictive quality models.

items.text: How well do product evaluations cover both interim and final work products?

rubric.initial: Only final deliverables are reviewed, if anything.
rubric.managed: Major interim products receive some quality review.
rubric.defined: Both interim and final work products are evaluated per defined quality plans with appropriate criteria.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Phase-specific quality metrics are quantitatively tracked with early quality indicators driving intervention.
rubric.optimizing: Comprehensive product quality monitoring uses continuous assessment with intelligent early warning systems.

items.text: How effectively are product evaluation results used to improve development practices?

rubric.initial: Product evaluation results are not used for improvement.
rubric.managed: Major product quality issues occasionally lead to process changes.
rubric.defined: Product evaluation findings systematically drive improvements to development standards and practices.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Quantitative analysis of product quality trends identifies root causes in development practices.
rubric.optimizing: Continuous product quality improvement uses automated analysis to refine development practices proactively.

items.text: How effectively are noncompliance issues identified and resolved?

rubric.initial: Noncompliance is not identified or is ignored.
rubric.managed: Major noncompliance is addressed when discovered but resolution is informal.
rubric.defined: Defined noncompliance resolution procedures include escalation paths, timelines, and verification of resolution.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Noncompliance patterns are quantitatively analyzed to identify systemic issues and drive preventive action.
rubric.optimizing: Noncompliance management is continuously improved with predictive detection and proactive prevention.

items.text: How well are escalation procedures used when noncompliance cannot be resolved at the project level?

rubric.initial: No escalation procedures exist for noncompliance.
rubric.managed: Major noncompliance is escalated informally to management.
rubric.defined: Defined escalation procedures ensure timely resolution of noncompliance at appropriate organizational levels.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Escalation effectiveness is quantitatively measured by resolution time and outcome quality.
rubric.optimizing: Intelligent escalation automatically routes noncompliance to the optimal resolution authority with full context.

items.text: How effectively does the organization prevent noncompliance recurrence?

rubric.initial: Noncompliance recurs without investigation into root causes.
rubric.managed: Major noncompliance events sometimes lead to corrective actions.
rubric.defined: Root cause analysis of noncompliance patterns drives systemic preventive improvements.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Noncompliance prevention effectiveness is quantitatively tracked with recurrence rate metrics.
rubric.optimizing: Proactive noncompliance prevention uses predictive analytics to identify at-risk areas and intervene early.

items.text: How effectively are quality audits planned and scheduled?

rubric.initial: No audit planning exists; quality checks are random or nonexistent.
rubric.managed: Basic audit schedules exist for major projects.
rubric.defined: Comprehensive audit plans are developed annually with risk-based scheduling and resource allocation.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Audit plan effectiveness is quantitatively measured by finding significance and coverage optimization.
rubric.optimizing: Audit planning is continuously optimized with risk-adaptive scheduling and predictive coverage analysis.

items.text: How well are audit scope and objectives defined for each engagement?

rubric.initial: Audit scope and objectives are undefined.
rubric.managed: Basic scope is communicated but objectives are vague.
rubric.defined: Clear audit objectives, scope, criteria, and methods are defined and communicated for each engagement.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Audit scope optimization is data-driven using risk analysis and historical finding patterns.
rubric.optimizing: Intelligent audit scoping automatically adjusts focus areas based on real-time risk and change data.

items.text: How effectively are audit resources and competencies managed?

rubric.initial: No dedicated audit resources or competency requirements exist.
rubric.managed: Some auditor training is provided but competency management is informal.
rubric.defined: Auditor competency requirements are defined with training programs and certification criteria.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Auditor effectiveness is quantitatively measured and used to optimize team composition and training investments.
rubric.optimizing: Audit resource management is continuously improved with competency analytics and intelligent auditor assignment.

items.text: How effectively are quality metrics defined and collected?

rubric.initial: No quality metrics are defined or collected.
rubric.managed: Basic metrics (defect counts) are collected for major projects.
rubric.defined: Comprehensive quality metrics are defined, consistently collected, and aligned with quality objectives.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Metrics validity and predictive value are quantitatively assessed and optimized.
rubric.optimizing: Quality metrics are continuously evolved using outcome correlation analysis and industry benchmarking.

items.text: How well are quality metrics analyzed and used for decision-making?

rubric.initial: Quality metrics are not analyzed or used for decisions.
rubric.managed: Basic metric summaries are reviewed periodically.
rubric.defined: Regular quality metric analysis informs decisions about process changes, resource allocation, and risk management.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Advanced statistical analysis of quality metrics provides quantitative decision support.
rubric.optimizing: Predictive quality analytics enable proactive decision-making and continuous quality optimization.

items.text: How effectively are quality metrics communicated to stakeholders?

rubric.initial: Quality metrics are not shared with stakeholders.
rubric.managed: Basic quality summaries are included in project reports.
rubric.defined: Quality dashboards provide relevant metrics to all stakeholder levels with appropriate context.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Stakeholder-specific quality reports are quantitatively tailored to decision needs.
rubric.optimizing: Intelligent quality communication automatically surfaces actionable insights tailored to each stakeholder.

items.text: How effectively are organizational and project training needs identified?

rubric.initial: Training needs are not assessed; training is ad hoc or reactive.
rubric.managed: Basic training needs are identified for new tools or major process changes.
rubric.defined: Systematic training needs analysis aligns skill requirements with organizational objectives and project demands.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Training needs are quantitatively validated using competency assessments and performance gap analysis.
rubric.optimizing: Training needs identification is continuously optimized with predictive workforce analytics and strategic capability planning.

items.text: How well are individual competency gaps identified and addressed?

rubric.initial: Individual competency gaps are unknown.
rubric.managed: Managers informally identify skill gaps for their direct reports.
rubric.defined: Formal competency frameworks with individual assessment and development planning are in place.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Competency assessment accuracy is quantitatively validated against performance outcomes.
rubric.optimizing: Personalized learning paths are automatically generated and adapted based on performance data and career goals.

items.text: How effectively are training priorities aligned with strategic objectives?

rubric.initial: Training has no connection to strategy.
rubric.managed: Some training investments loosely relate to strategic goals.
rubric.defined: Training priorities are explicitly derived from strategic plans with documented alignment rationale.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Training-strategy alignment effectiveness is quantitatively measured by capability development and business outcomes.
rubric.optimizing: Dynamic training prioritization continuously adjusts to evolving strategic priorities with predictive capability modeling.

items.text: How well are training programs designed and developed?

rubric.initial: No formal training programs exist; learning is entirely on the job.
rubric.managed: Basic training materials are developed for critical topics.
rubric.defined: Comprehensive training programs with learning objectives, curricula, materials, and assessments are developed.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Training program quality is quantitatively measured using instructional design effectiveness metrics.
rubric.optimizing: Training programs are continuously evolved using learning analytics and adaptive instructional design.

items.text: How effectively are training materials maintained and updated?

rubric.initial: Training materials are outdated or nonexistent.
rubric.managed: Materials are updated occasionally for major changes.
rubric.defined: Defined maintenance procedures ensure training materials are regularly reviewed and updated.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Material currency and accuracy are quantitatively tracked with automated update triggers.
rubric.optimizing: Training content is dynamically maintained with automated updates from process and tool changes.

items.text: How well are training programs validated before deployment?

rubric.initial: Training programs are deployed without validation.
rubric.managed: Basic reviews of training content occur before major deployments.
rubric.defined: Pilot programs and formal reviews validate training effectiveness before organization-wide deployment.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Validation effectiveness is quantitatively measured by comparing pilot and full deployment outcomes.
rubric.optimizing: Continuous training validation uses real-time learning analytics to optimize programs before and during deployment.

items.text: How effectively does the organization use diverse training delivery methods?

rubric.initial: Only informal on-the-job training is available.
rubric.managed: Classroom training is available for some topics.
rubric.defined: Multiple delivery methods (classroom, e-learning, mentoring, simulation) are used based on topic and audience needs.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Delivery method effectiveness is quantitatively compared using learning outcome and efficiency metrics.
rubric.optimizing: Delivery method selection is continuously optimized using learning analytics and personalized delivery recommendations.

items.text: How well does the organization leverage technology for training delivery?

rubric.initial: No technology-enabled training exists.
rubric.managed: Basic e-learning modules are available for some topics.
rubric.defined: A comprehensive learning management system supports diverse technology-enabled delivery methods.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Technology platform effectiveness is quantitatively measured by engagement, completion, and learning outcome metrics.
rubric.optimizing: Advanced learning technologies (VR, adaptive learning, microlearning) are continuously evaluated and adopted.

items.text: How effectively is just-in-time training provided when needed?

rubric.initial: Training is only available through scheduled sessions; no on-demand options exist.
rubric.managed: Some reference materials are available for self-study.
rubric.defined: Just-in-time training resources including job aids, quick references, and on-demand modules are available.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Just-in-time training usage and effectiveness are quantitatively tracked with impact on performance metrics.
rubric.optimizing: Intelligent performance support systems deliver contextually relevant training at the point of need.

items.text: How effectively is training effectiveness evaluated?

rubric.initial: Training effectiveness is not evaluated.
rubric.managed: Basic satisfaction surveys are collected after training sessions.
rubric.defined: Multi-level evaluation (reaction, learning, behavior, results) is applied using the Kirkpatrick model or equivalent.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Training ROI is quantitatively calculated with statistical analysis of performance improvement attribution.
rubric.optimizing: Training effectiveness evaluation is continuously refined using advanced analytics and longitudinal outcome tracking.

items.text: How well are training evaluation results used to improve programs?

rubric.initial: Evaluation results are not used for improvement.
rubric.managed: Low satisfaction scores occasionally lead to content revisions.
rubric.defined: Systematic analysis of evaluation results drives specific improvements to training content, delivery, and design.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Quantitative improvement targets are set and tracked for training programs based on outcome data.
rubric.optimizing: Continuous training improvement uses real-time analytics to adapt programs dynamically during delivery.

items.text: How effectively does the organization measure the business impact of training?

rubric.initial: No attempt is made to measure training's business impact.
rubric.managed: Anecdotal evidence of training benefits is occasionally cited.
rubric.defined: Defined metrics link training outcomes to business performance indicators.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Statistical analysis quantifies training's contribution to productivity, quality, and business objectives.
rubric.optimizing: Predictive models optimize training investment by forecasting business impact of different training options.

items.text: How effectively does the organization capture and share organizational knowledge?

rubric.initial: Knowledge exists only in individuals' heads; there is no capture or sharing mechanism.
rubric.managed: Some documentation exists but knowledge sharing is informal and inconsistent.
rubric.defined: Formal knowledge management processes with repositories, taxonomies, and sharing incentives are in place.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Knowledge utilization is quantitatively measured with impact analysis on project outcomes.
rubric.optimizing: Knowledge management is continuously optimized with AI-powered knowledge discovery and intelligent recommendation.

items.text: How well is tacit knowledge converted to explicit organizational assets?

rubric.initial: No effort is made to capture tacit knowledge.
rubric.managed: Key experts occasionally document their knowledge.
rubric.defined: Systematic programs (mentoring, communities of practice, knowledge capture sessions) convert tacit to explicit knowledge.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Tacit knowledge capture effectiveness is quantitatively measured by knowledge retention and reuse rates.
rubric.optimizing: Advanced knowledge capture techniques continuously extract and codify tacit knowledge into organizational assets.

items.text: How effectively is knowledge retained when employees depart?

rubric.initial: Critical knowledge is lost when employees leave.
rubric.managed: Basic handover procedures exist for key roles.
rubric.defined: Comprehensive knowledge retention programs include succession planning, documentation, and transition processes.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Knowledge retention effectiveness is quantitatively measured by post-departure impact on team performance.
rubric.optimizing: Predictive retention risk models trigger proactive knowledge capture for critical knowledge holders.

items.text: How well-defined is the organization's governance structure for projects and programs?

rubric.initial: No governance structure exists; decision-making authority is unclear.
rubric.managed: Basic management oversight exists but governance roles and responsibilities are undefined.
rubric.defined: Defined governance structure with clear roles, responsibilities, authority levels, and accountability frameworks.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Governance structure effectiveness is quantitatively measured by decision quality and organizational performance.
rubric.optimizing: Governance structure is continuously optimized based on organizational performance data and emerging best practices.

items.text: How effectively do governance bodies function?

rubric.initial: No governance bodies are established.
rubric.managed: Governance bodies exist but meet irregularly with unclear mandates.
rubric.defined: Governance bodies have defined charters, membership, meeting cadences, and decision-making procedures.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Governance body effectiveness is quantitatively evaluated using decision quality and stakeholder satisfaction metrics.
rubric.optimizing: Governance bodies are continuously improved with data-driven meeting optimization and decision effectiveness analysis.

items.text: How well is governance authority distributed across organizational levels?

rubric.initial: All decisions are centralized regardless of significance, or no governance authority exists.
rubric.managed: Some delegation exists but authority boundaries are unclear.
rubric.defined: Defined authority matrices specify decision rights at each organizational level with clear escalation paths.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Authority distribution effectiveness is quantitatively analyzed using decision cycle time and quality metrics.
rubric.optimizing: Dynamic authority distribution adapts to organizational context with optimized delegation based on decision analytics.

items.text: How effectively are organizational policies defined and maintained?

rubric.initial: No formal policies exist; practices vary across the organization.
rubric.managed: Basic policies exist for critical areas but are not comprehensive or consistently maintained.
rubric.defined: Comprehensive policy framework with defined lifecycle, ownership, review cycles, and version control is in place.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Policy effectiveness is quantitatively measured by compliance rates and outcome correlation.
rubric.optimizing: Policy management is continuously optimized with automated compliance monitoring and adaptive policy evolution.

items.text: How well are policies communicated and understood across the organization?

rubric.initial: Policies are documented but not effectively communicated; awareness is low.
rubric.managed: Major policies are communicated during onboarding and major changes.
rubric.defined: Systematic policy communication ensures awareness through multiple channels with comprehension verification.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Policy awareness and understanding are quantitatively measured with gap-targeted communication campaigns.
rubric.optimizing: Intelligent policy communication personalizes content delivery and validates comprehension automatically.

items.text: How effectively is policy compliance monitored and enforced?

rubric.initial: Policy compliance is not monitored.
rubric.managed: Compliance is checked reactively when issues arise.
rubric.defined: Regular compliance monitoring with defined procedures, reporting, and enforcement mechanisms is in place.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Compliance levels are quantitatively tracked with root cause analysis of noncompliance patterns.
rubric.optimizing: Automated compliance monitoring with predictive noncompliance detection and proactive intervention.

items.text: How effectively does governance provide oversight of project and program performance?

rubric.initial: No performance oversight exists; projects operate without governance visibility.
rubric.managed: Basic project status is reported to management periodically.
rubric.defined: Defined oversight mechanisms include regular reviews, gate decisions, and performance dashboards.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Oversight effectiveness is quantitatively measured by early issue detection and intervention success rates.
rubric.optimizing: Oversight mechanisms are continuously optimized with predictive performance models and automated intervention triggers.

items.text: How well do oversight reviews drive timely and effective decisions?

rubric.initial: Reviews occur but rarely result in decisions or actions.
rubric.managed: Major issues identified in reviews sometimes lead to decisions.
rubric.defined: Review processes have defined decision criteria with documented actions and follow-up tracking.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Review decision quality is quantitatively measured by outcome analysis and action completion rates.
rubric.optimizing: Intelligent oversight reviews prioritize decision items and provide AI-assisted decision recommendations.

items.text: How effectively does governance adapt oversight intensity to risk and complexity?

rubric.initial: Same oversight applies to all projects regardless of risk or complexity.
rubric.managed: High-profile projects receive more attention but criteria for differentiation are informal.
rubric.defined: Risk-based oversight frameworks define appropriate governance intensity based on project characteristics.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Oversight intensity optimization is quantitatively validated using project outcome analysis.
rubric.optimizing: Adaptive oversight dynamically adjusts governance intensity based on real-time risk and performance data.

items.text: How effectively does the organization manage regulatory and standards compliance?

rubric.initial: Regulatory compliance is not systematically managed.
rubric.managed: Basic compliance awareness exists for major regulations.
rubric.defined: Comprehensive compliance management covers identification, assessment, implementation, and monitoring of requirements.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Compliance status is quantitatively tracked with automated gap analysis and trend reporting.
rubric.optimizing: Compliance management is continuously optimized with regulatory change monitoring and predictive impact analysis.

items.text: How well are compliance requirements integrated into project processes?

rubric.initial: Compliance is an afterthought addressed late in the project lifecycle.
rubric.managed: Major compliance requirements are considered during planning.
rubric.defined: Compliance requirements are systematically integrated into project processes from inception through delivery.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Integration effectiveness is quantitatively measured by compliance issue rates by lifecycle phase.
rubric.optimizing: Seamless compliance integration uses automated requirement mapping and continuous compliance verification.

items.text: How effectively are compliance audit findings addressed?

rubric.initial: Compliance audit findings are ignored or deprioritized.
rubric.managed: Major findings are addressed but tracking is informal.
rubric.defined: All findings are tracked with defined remediation plans, owners, deadlines, and verification procedures.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Finding resolution effectiveness is quantitatively analyzed with trend and root cause analysis.
rubric.optimizing: Proactive compliance management uses predictive analytics to prevent findings before audits occur.

items.text: How effectively does governance engage relevant stakeholders in decision-making?

rubric.initial: Stakeholders are not engaged; decisions are made in isolation.
rubric.managed: Key stakeholders are consulted on major decisions informally.
rubric.defined: Defined stakeholder engagement processes ensure appropriate participation in governance activities.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Engagement effectiveness is quantitatively measured by stakeholder satisfaction and decision implementation success.
rubric.optimizing: Stakeholder engagement is continuously optimized using sentiment analysis and engagement impact modeling.

items.text: How well does governance ensure transparency and communication?

rubric.initial: Governance decisions are opaque; stakeholders lack visibility.
rubric.managed: Major decisions are communicated through management channels.
rubric.defined: Governance transparency is ensured through defined communication procedures, published minutes, and accessible records.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Transparency effectiveness is quantitatively measured by stakeholder awareness and trust metrics.
rubric.optimizing: Intelligent governance communication automatically tailors transparency to stakeholder needs and preferences.

items.text: How effectively does governance balance diverse stakeholder interests?

rubric.initial: Dominant stakeholders' interests prevail without consideration of others.
rubric.managed: Attempts are made to balance interests but processes are informal.
rubric.defined: Defined frameworks ensure diverse stakeholder interests are considered with documented trade-off rationale.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Stakeholder interest balance is quantitatively assessed using multi-criteria analysis and fairness metrics.
rubric.optimizing: Dynamic stakeholder interest management uses advanced analytics to optimize value creation across all parties.

items.text: How well does the organization's process infrastructure support defined processes?

rubric.initial: No process infrastructure exists; processes are ad hoc and unsupported.
rubric.managed: Basic process documentation is available but infrastructure support is minimal.
rubric.defined: Comprehensive process infrastructure includes documentation, workflow automation, and access systems.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Infrastructure utilization and effectiveness are quantitatively measured with user satisfaction and adoption metrics.
rubric.optimizing: Process infrastructure is continuously optimized with AI-assisted process navigation and contextual guidance.

items.text: How effectively does the process infrastructure support process tailoring?

rubric.initial: Tailoring is not supported by infrastructure.
rubric.managed: Basic templates allow some customization.
rubric.defined: Infrastructure provides configurable process frameworks with tailoring guides and automated customization.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Tailoring support effectiveness is quantitatively measured by adoption rates and tailoring quality metrics.
rubric.optimizing: Intelligent tailoring assistance recommends optimal configurations based on project characteristics and outcomes data.

items.text: How well does process infrastructure integrate across organizational functions?

rubric.initial: Process infrastructure is siloed with no integration across functions.
rubric.managed: Some integration exists between closely related functions.
rubric.defined: Integrated process infrastructure provides seamless cross-functional workflow support.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Integration effectiveness is quantitatively measured by cross-functional process efficiency and error rates.
rubric.optimizing: Continuously optimized integration uses API-driven architecture and intelligent workflow orchestration.

items.text: How effectively does the organization's tool environment support project and process needs?

rubric.initial: Tools are selected ad hoc; there is no coordinated tool environment.
rubric.managed: Basic tools are provided for major functions but integration is poor.
rubric.defined: A coordinated tool environment with defined tool standards, integration, and support is in place.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Tool environment effectiveness is quantitatively measured by productivity, quality, and user satisfaction metrics.
rubric.optimizing: Tool environment is continuously optimized through usage analytics and emerging technology evaluation.

items.text: How well are tools integrated to support end-to-end workflows?

rubric.initial: Tools are not integrated; data is manually transferred between systems.
rubric.managed: Basic integrations exist between a few key tools.
rubric.defined: Comprehensive tool integration supports end-to-end data flow across the lifecycle with defined interface standards.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Integration quality is quantitatively measured by data consistency, transfer time, and error rates.
rubric.optimizing: Self-healing tool integration with automated data reconciliation and intelligent workflow routing.

items.text: How effectively is the tool environment maintained and evolved?

rubric.initial: Tools are rarely updated; outdated versions persist.
rubric.managed: Major tool updates are deployed but maintenance is reactive.
rubric.defined: Defined tool lifecycle management includes evaluation, deployment, maintenance, and retirement procedures.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Tool health metrics are quantitatively tracked with proactive maintenance scheduling.
rubric.optimizing: Intelligent tool management continuously evaluates, updates, and optimizes the tool portfolio.

items.text: How well does the measurement infrastructure support data collection and analysis?

rubric.initial: No measurement infrastructure exists; data collection is manual and inconsistent.
rubric.managed: Basic spreadsheet-based data collection exists for major metrics.
rubric.defined: Automated measurement infrastructure collects, stores, and analyzes data from multiple sources.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Measurement infrastructure quality is quantitatively assessed with data accuracy and timeliness metrics.
rubric.optimizing: Measurement infrastructure is continuously enhanced with automated anomaly detection and intelligent visualization.

items.text: How effectively are measurement definitions standardized and maintained?

rubric.initial: No standard measurement definitions exist; metrics mean different things to different people.
rubric.managed: Basic definitions exist for key metrics but consistency is not ensured.
rubric.defined: Comprehensive measurement dictionary with standard definitions, collection procedures, and ownership is maintained.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Definition consistency is quantitatively verified through automated validation and cross-reference checking.
rubric.optimizing: Measurement definitions are continuously refined using data quality analysis and industry standard alignment.

items.text: How well does measurement infrastructure support organizational decision-making?

rubric.initial: Measurement data is not available to support decisions.
rubric.managed: Basic reports provide some decision support for major reviews.
rubric.defined: Measurement infrastructure provides dashboards and analytics that directly support defined decision processes.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Decision support effectiveness is quantitatively measured by decision quality correlation.
rubric.optimizing: Intelligent decision support uses predictive analytics and automated insight generation from measurement data.

items.text: How well-defined are organizational standards for processes and work products?

rubric.initial: No organizational standards exist.
rubric.managed: Basic standards exist for critical processes.
rubric.defined: Comprehensive organizational standards cover processes, work products, tools, and methods with governance procedures.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Standards effectiveness is quantitatively measured by adoption rates and outcome quality correlation.
rubric.optimizing: Standards are continuously evolved using effectiveness analytics and industry best practice monitoring.

items.text: How effectively are standards communicated and adopted across the organization?

rubric.initial: Standards, if they exist, are not communicated; adoption is minimal.
rubric.managed: Standards are published but adoption depends on individual initiative.
rubric.defined: Systematic deployment of standards includes training, support, and adoption tracking.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Adoption rates and compliance levels are quantitatively tracked with targeted interventions for lagging areas.
rubric.optimizing: Standards adoption is continuously optimized with intelligent deployment strategies and personalized support.

items.text: How effectively are standards maintained and updated?

rubric.initial: Standards are static and become obsolete quickly.
rubric.managed: Major standards are updated occasionally but maintenance is reactive.
rubric.defined: Defined review cycles and update procedures keep standards current and relevant.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Standards currency is quantitatively tracked with automated staleness detection and update prioritization.
rubric.optimizing: Continuous standards maintenance uses automated change detection and intelligent update recommendation.

items.text: How well does the organization maintain a process asset library?

rubric.initial: No process asset library exists.
rubric.managed: Some process documents are stored in shared drives but not organized or curated.
rubric.defined: A managed process asset library with defined structure, ownership, and access controls is maintained.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Library utilization and asset quality are quantitatively tracked with effectiveness metrics.
rubric.optimizing: Process asset library is continuously optimized with intelligent search, recommendation, and automated curation.

items.text: How effectively is the process asset library used by projects?

rubric.initial: Projects do not use organizational process assets.
rubric.managed: Some projects reference organizational assets when easily accessible.
rubric.defined: Process asset usage is standard practice with defined adoption procedures and support.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Usage rates and impact on project outcomes are quantitatively measured and correlated.
rubric.optimizing: Intelligent asset recommendation surfaces relevant assets automatically based on project context.

items.text: How well are process assets curated for quality and relevance?

rubric.initial: No curation occurs; assets become stale and unreliable.
rubric.managed: Major assets are occasionally reviewed for accuracy.
rubric.defined: Defined curation procedures ensure assets are reviewed, updated, and archived on regular cycles.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Asset quality metrics (freshness, accuracy, usage) are quantitatively tracked with automated alerts.
rubric.optimizing: AI-assisted curation continuously evaluates and improves asset quality based on usage patterns and feedback.

items.text: How well are standard processes defined and documented?

rubric.initial: No standard processes are defined; every project operates differently.
rubric.managed: Basic process descriptions exist for critical activities.
rubric.defined: Comprehensive standard processes are defined with activities, roles, inputs, outputs, and quality criteria.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Process quality metrics are quantitatively tracked with effectiveness analysis driving improvements.
rubric.optimizing: Standard processes are continuously enhanced using process mining and outcome correlation analysis.

items.text: How effectively are standard processes deployed across the organization?

rubric.initial: No deployment of standard processes occurs.
rubric.managed: Some processes are mandated but deployment support is minimal.
rubric.defined: Systematic process deployment includes training, tools, mentoring, and adoption monitoring.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Deployment effectiveness is quantitatively measured by adoption speed, consistency, and performance impact.
rubric.optimizing: Intelligent process deployment adapts rollout strategies based on organizational readiness and adoption analytics.

items.text: How well do standard processes accommodate diverse project types?

rubric.initial: One-size-fits-all processes are rigid and inappropriate for many projects.
rubric.managed: Some informal adaptation occurs but without guidelines.
rubric.defined: Standard processes include tailoring guidelines for different project types, sizes, and methodologies.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Tailoring effectiveness is quantitatively validated by comparing outcomes across tailoring configurations.
rubric.optimizing: Adaptive standard processes automatically configure themselves based on project characteristics and historical outcomes.

items.text: How well-defined are tailoring guidelines for standard processes?

rubric.initial: No tailoring guidelines exist; adaptation is arbitrary.
rubric.managed: Basic guidance on optional vs. mandatory process elements exists.
rubric.defined: Comprehensive tailoring guidelines specify criteria, permitted variations, and approval requirements.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Tailoring guideline effectiveness is quantitatively validated by correlating tailoring decisions with project outcomes.
rubric.optimizing: Tailoring guidelines are continuously evolved using outcome analytics and project characteristic analysis.

items.text: How effectively are tailoring decisions documented and reviewed?

rubric.initial: Tailoring decisions are not documented.
rubric.managed: Major tailoring decisions are noted but not systematically reviewed.
rubric.defined: All tailoring decisions are documented with rationale and subject to defined review and approval.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Tailoring decision quality is quantitatively assessed through outcome comparison analysis.
rubric.optimizing: Automated tailoring documentation with intelligent review routing optimizes decision quality and cycle time.

items.text: How well does the organization learn from tailoring experience to improve guidelines?

rubric.initial: No learning from tailoring experience occurs.
rubric.managed: Occasional feedback on tailoring is received but not incorporated.
rubric.defined: Systematic collection and analysis of tailoring experience drives guideline improvements.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Quantitative analysis of tailoring outcomes drives evidence-based guideline refinement.
rubric.optimizing: Continuous tailoring optimization uses machine learning on project outcomes to recommend ideal configurations.

items.text: How effectively are lessons learned captured across the organization?

rubric.initial: Lessons learned are not captured.
rubric.managed: Post-project lessons are occasionally documented but inconsistently.
rubric.defined: Systematic lessons learned capture occurs at defined project milestones with standardized formats.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Lessons learned capture rates and quality are quantitatively tracked with impact analysis.
rubric.optimizing: Continuous lessons learned capture with real-time knowledge extraction and intelligent categorization.

items.text: How well are lessons learned disseminated and applied to future projects?

rubric.initial: Lessons, if captured, are filed and forgotten.
rubric.managed: Major lessons are shared in project kickoffs occasionally.
rubric.defined: Defined dissemination processes ensure relevant lessons reach future projects with application tracking.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Lesson application rates and impact on project outcomes are quantitatively measured.
rubric.optimizing: Intelligent lesson recommendation automatically surfaces relevant lessons at decision points in project workflows.

items.text: How effectively do lessons learned drive organizational process improvements?

rubric.initial: No connection between lessons learned and process improvement exists.
rubric.managed: Major recurring issues sometimes trigger process changes.
rubric.defined: Systematic analysis of lessons learned patterns drives prioritized organizational process improvements.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Quantitative analysis of lesson themes and frequencies drives evidence-based improvement prioritization.
rubric.optimizing: Continuous improvement loops automatically translate lesson patterns into process enhancement proposals.

items.text: How effectively are best practices identified and documented?

rubric.initial: Best practices are not identified or documented.
rubric.managed: Some successful practices are informally recognized.
rubric.defined: Systematic best practice identification through benchmarking, practice reviews, and outcome analysis.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Best practice validation uses quantitative outcome analysis with statistical significance testing.
rubric.optimizing: Continuous best practice discovery uses automated outcome analysis and industry intelligence scanning.

items.text: How well are best practices disseminated across the organization?

rubric.initial: Best practices remain local to the teams that developed them.
rubric.managed: Some sharing occurs through informal networks.
rubric.defined: Formal best practice sharing mechanisms include communities of practice, workshops, and published guides.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Best practice adoption and impact are quantitatively tracked across the organization.
rubric.optimizing: Intelligent best practice recommendation systems proactively match practices to team contexts and needs.

items.text: How effectively are external best practices incorporated into organizational processes?

rubric.initial: External practices are not sought or considered.
rubric.managed: Some awareness of industry practices exists but incorporation is rare.
rubric.defined: Systematic external benchmarking and best practice evaluation drives process enhancement.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: External practice adoption effectiveness is quantitatively measured against internal outcome improvements.
rubric.optimizing: Continuous external intelligence gathering with automated relevance assessment and adoption recommendations.

items.text: How well are measurement objectives aligned with business and process goals?

rubric.initial: No measurement objectives exist; metrics are collected without purpose.
rubric.managed: Basic metrics are collected for project reporting without clear alignment to goals.
rubric.defined: Measurement objectives are derived from business and process goals using GQM or similar approaches.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Objective alignment is quantitatively validated through outcome correlation analysis.
rubric.optimizing: Measurement objective alignment is continuously refined using goal-outcome analytics and strategic planning data.

items.text: How effectively are measurement objectives communicated to stakeholders?

rubric.initial: Measurement objectives are not communicated.
rubric.managed: Basic metric definitions are available but objectives and context are unclear.
rubric.defined: Measurement objectives are clearly communicated with rationale and expected use to all stakeholders.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Communication effectiveness is quantitatively measured by stakeholder understanding and data quality.
rubric.optimizing: Intelligent measurement communication automatically tailors objective context to each stakeholder role.

items.text: How well are measurement objectives periodically reviewed and updated?

rubric.initial: Measurement objectives are never reviewed; they become irrelevant over time.
rubric.managed: Major measurement changes are made reactively when objectives clearly fail.
rubric.defined: Regular reviews ensure measurement objectives remain aligned with evolving business and process goals.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Review effectiveness is quantitatively measured by objective relevance and predictive value over time.
rubric.optimizing: Continuous measurement objective optimization uses real-time goal tracking and automated relevance assessment.

items.text: How effectively is measurement data collected across the organization?

rubric.initial: Data collection is inconsistent; metrics are unreliable.
rubric.managed: Basic data is collected manually for major projects.
rubric.defined: Automated data collection with defined procedures, quality checks, and standardized formats is in place.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Data collection quality and completeness are quantitatively measured with automated validation.
rubric.optimizing: Intelligent data collection automatically adapts to evolving measurement needs with quality assurance.

items.text: How well does the organization ensure data quality and integrity?

rubric.initial: Data quality is not managed; metrics are often inaccurate.
rubric.managed: Basic validation catches obvious errors in critical metrics.
rubric.defined: Comprehensive data quality management includes validation rules, anomaly detection, and quality reporting.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Data quality is quantitatively tracked with statistical process control and root cause analysis of issues.
rubric.optimizing: Self-healing data quality management with automated correction and continuous quality optimization.

items.text: How effectively is data collection burden minimized while maintaining quality?

rubric.initial: Data collection is burdensome; overhead deters compliance.
rubric.managed: Some automation reduces collection effort for major metrics.
rubric.defined: Collection processes are designed to minimize overhead through automation and integration with work tools.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Collection efficiency is quantitatively measured with continuous optimization of effort-to-value ratio.
rubric.optimizing: Zero-overhead data collection uses automated extraction from existing tools and workflows.

items.text: How effectively does the organization analyze measurement data?

rubric.initial: Data is collected but not analyzed.
rubric.managed: Basic descriptive statistics and charts are produced.
rubric.defined: Defined analysis techniques including trend analysis, comparison, and root cause analysis are consistently applied.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Advanced statistical analysis including hypothesis testing, regression, and multivariate analysis is standard.
rubric.optimizing: AI-driven analytics automatically discover patterns, anomalies, and causal relationships in measurement data.

items.text: How well are analysis results translated into actionable insights?

rubric.initial: Analysis does not produce actionable insights.
rubric.managed: Occasional insights emerge from analysis but actionability is inconsistent.
rubric.defined: Analysis procedures include defined steps for translating findings into specific recommendations and action items.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Insight quality and actionability are quantitatively measured by implementation rates and outcome impact.
rubric.optimizing: Automated insight generation with prioritized, context-specific recommendations optimizes decision support.

items.text: How effectively are analysis capabilities developed across the organization?

rubric.initial: No analysis capabilities exist beyond basic spreadsheet use.
rubric.managed: A few specialists have analysis skills.
rubric.defined: Analysis training and tools are provided to key roles with defined competency requirements.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Analysis capability maturity is quantitatively assessed with gap-targeted development programs.
rubric.optimizing: Organization-wide analytics capability is continuously developed through advanced training and AI augmentation.

items.text: How effectively are performance baselines established and maintained?

rubric.initial: No performance baselines exist.
rubric.managed: Basic historical averages are informally referenced as benchmarks.
rubric.defined: Performance baselines are statistically established from historical data with defined update procedures.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Baselines are maintained with statistical process control and used for quantitative management.
rubric.optimizing: Dynamic baselines automatically adjust using real-time data and contextual factors for optimal relevance.

items.text: How well are baselines used for performance prediction and management?
