rubric.initial: Baselines are not used for prediction.
rubric.managed: Past performance is informally referenced when setting expectations.
rubric.defined: Baselines are systematically used for setting performance targets and identifying deviations.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Statistical prediction models based on baselines provide confidence intervals for performance forecasting.
rubric.optimizing: Predictive performance management uses dynamic baselines and machine learning for accurate forecasting.

items.text: How effectively are baselines shared and used consistently across the organization?

rubric.initial: No organizational baselines exist; each project defines its own benchmarks.
rubric.managed: Some organizational averages are known informally.
rubric.defined: Organizational baselines are published and required for project planning and performance management.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Baseline applicability is quantitatively validated for different project contexts with segmented baselines.
rubric.optimizing: Context-adaptive baselines automatically provide relevant benchmarks based on project characteristics.

items.text: How effectively does the organization apply statistical methods to process management?

rubric.initial: No statistical methods are used in process management.
rubric.managed: Basic statistics are used for reporting (averages, counts).
rubric.defined: Statistical process control and analysis techniques are defined and applied to key processes.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Advanced statistical methods including multivariate analysis and process capability studies are standard.
rubric.optimizing: State-of-the-art statistical and machine learning methods are continuously evaluated and applied.

items.text: How well does the organization understand and manage process variation?

rubric.initial: Process variation is not recognized or understood.
rubric.managed: Some awareness of variation exists but it is not systematically managed.
rubric.defined: Common and special cause variation are identified and managed using defined statistical techniques.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Process capability indices are tracked with quantitative targets and improvement actions.
rubric.optimizing: Advanced variation management uses real-time statistical monitoring with automated intervention triggers.

items.text: How effectively does the organization build statistical literacy across relevant roles?

rubric.initial: No statistical literacy exists beyond basic arithmetic.
rubric.managed: A few specialists understand statistical methods.
rubric.defined: Statistical training is provided to relevant roles with defined competency requirements.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Statistical literacy is quantitatively assessed with targeted development programs for gaps.
rubric.optimizing: Continuous statistical capability development uses adaptive learning and AI-assisted analysis support.

items.text: How well are organizational processes defined and documented?

rubric.initial: Processes are not defined; work is performed differently each time.
rubric.managed: Basic process descriptions exist for critical activities.
rubric.defined: Comprehensive process definitions with roles, activities, inputs, outputs, and quality criteria are maintained.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Process definition quality is quantitatively assessed using adherence and outcome correlation metrics.
rubric.optimizing: Process definitions are continuously optimized using process mining and automated quality assessment.

items.text: How effectively are process interfaces and dependencies managed?

rubric.initial: Process interfaces are not defined; handoff issues are common.
rubric.managed: Major process interfaces are informally understood.
rubric.defined: Process interfaces are formally defined with documented handoffs, triggers, and quality criteria.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Interface effectiveness is quantitatively measured by handoff error rates and cycle time impact.
rubric.optimizing: Process interface management is optimized with automated monitoring and intelligent workflow orchestration.

items.text: How well are processes documented at appropriate levels of detail?

rubric.initial: Processes are either undocumented or documented at inappropriate detail levels.
rubric.managed: Major processes have high-level documentation.
rubric.defined: Process documentation follows defined standards with appropriate detail levels for different audiences.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Documentation quality and usability are quantitatively measured by user effectiveness and satisfaction.
rubric.optimizing: Adaptive process documentation automatically adjusts detail level based on user role and context.

items.text: How effectively are processes deployed across the organization?

rubric.quantitatively-managed: Communication effectiveness is quantitatively measured by stakeholder satisfaction and information timeliness.
rubric.optimizing: Automated incident communication with personalized updates and predictive resolution timeline sharing.

items.text: How effectively does the organization prevent incident recurrence?

rubric.initial: No prevention efforts exist; the same incidents recur repeatedly.
rubric.managed: Major recurring incidents sometimes trigger corrective action.
rubric.defined: Systematic problem management translates incident patterns into preventive changes.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Prevention effectiveness is quantitatively measured by recurrence rate reduction and cost avoidance.
rubric.optimizing: Proactive incident prevention uses predictive analytics to prevent incidents before they occur.

items.text: How well does the organization learn from incidents to improve services?

rubric.initial: No learning from incidents occurs.
rubric.managed: Major incidents generate lessons learned that are occasionally referenced.
rubric.defined: Post-incident reviews systematically capture lessons that drive service improvements.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Improvement impact is quantitatively measured with attribution analysis.
rubric.optimizing: Continuous incident-driven improvement uses automated learning extraction and improvement deployment.

items.text: How effectively is incident knowledge captured and made accessible?

rubric.initial: No incident knowledge base exists.
rubric.managed: Some resolution notes are stored in ticketing systems.
rubric.defined: A structured knowledge base with searchable articles, resolution guides, and known errors is maintained.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Knowledge base utilization and effectiveness are quantitatively measured with impact on resolution times.
rubric.optimizing: Intelligent knowledge management with AI-powered search and automated article generation from resolutions.

items.text: How well is the knowledge base maintained and kept current?

rubric.initial: Knowledge, if stored, becomes outdated quickly.
rubric.managed: Major articles are occasionally updated.
rubric.defined: Defined maintenance procedures ensure knowledge articles are reviewed, updated, and retired systematically.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Knowledge freshness and accuracy are quantitatively tracked with automated staleness detection.
rubric.optimizing: Self-maintaining knowledge base with automated updates from incident patterns and resolution outcomes.

items.text: How effectively is capacity planning performed for services?

rubric.initial: No capacity planning occurs; capacity issues are discovered during outages.
rubric.managed: Basic capacity monitoring exists for critical infrastructure.
rubric.defined: Comprehensive capacity planning with demand forecasting and capacity modeling for all services.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Capacity plans are quantitatively validated with statistical forecasting and scenario analysis.
rubric.optimizing: Dynamic capacity management uses AI-driven forecasting and automated scaling for optimal resource utilization.

items.text: How well are capacity requirements derived from business demand?

rubric.initial: Capacity decisions are based on technical defaults without business input.
rubric.managed: Major capacity decisions consider basic business growth projections.
rubric.defined: Systematic capacity requirement analysis translates business demand forecasts into technical capacity needs.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Demand-to-capacity models are quantitatively validated with accuracy tracking and refinement.
rubric.optimizing: Intelligent demand-capacity alignment uses real-time business signals for continuous capacity optimization.

items.text: How effectively are capacity risks identified and managed?

rubric.initial: Capacity risks are not identified until they cause service degradation.
rubric.managed: Major capacity constraints are known but risk management is informal.
rubric.defined: Systematic capacity risk assessment with monitoring and mitigation planning.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Capacity risk models are quantitatively analyzed with probability-weighted impact assessment.
rubric.optimizing: Predictive capacity risk management prevents issues through proactive scaling and resource rebalancing.

items.text: How well are availability targets defined and managed?

rubric.initial: No availability targets exist; uptime is not tracked.
rubric.managed: Basic uptime targets exist for critical services.
rubric.defined: Comprehensive availability targets are defined for all services based on business requirements.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Availability achievement is quantitatively tracked with trend analysis and predictive modeling.
rubric.optimizing: Dynamic availability management continuously optimizes targets and resource allocation for cost-effective reliability.

items.text: How effectively are availability dependencies and single points of failure managed?

rubric.initial: Dependencies and single points of failure are unknown.
rubric.managed: Major single points of failure are informally recognized.
rubric.defined: Systematic dependency analysis identifies and addresses single points of failure with redundancy planning.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Dependency risk is quantitatively modeled with failure probability and cascade impact analysis.
rubric.optimizing: Automated dependency management with continuous SPOF detection and proactive redundancy optimization.

items.text: How effectively is service performance monitored in real-time?

rubric.initial: No real-time performance monitoring exists.
rubric.managed: Basic monitoring covers system-level metrics for critical infrastructure.
rubric.defined: Comprehensive monitoring covers infrastructure, application, and service-level performance metrics.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Performance data is statistically analyzed with automated anomaly detection and alerting.
rubric.optimizing: Intelligent performance monitoring uses AI-driven analytics for predictive issue detection and auto-remediation.

items.text: How well are monitoring thresholds and alerts configured?

rubric.initial: No monitoring thresholds or alerts exist.
rubric.managed: Basic alerts exist for critical system failures.
rubric.defined: Comprehensive alerting with defined thresholds, escalation procedures, and noise reduction.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Alert effectiveness is quantitatively measured with false positive reduction and detection accuracy.
rubric.optimizing: Self-tuning alert systems continuously optimize thresholds based on pattern analysis and operational feedback.

items.text: How effectively is monitoring data used for capacity and availability improvement?

rubric.initial: Monitoring data is not analyzed for improvement.
rubric.managed: Major performance issues are reactively investigated.
rubric.defined: Systematic analysis of monitoring data drives capacity and availability improvement initiatives.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Advanced analytics identify optimization opportunities with quantitative impact projections.
rubric.optimizing: AI-driven monitoring analysis continuously identifies and implements availability and capacity optimizations.

items.text: How effectively does the organization optimize service capacity and availability?

rubric.initial: No optimization activities occur; resources are wasted or insufficient.
rubric.managed: Major performance issues trigger reactive optimization.
rubric.defined: Proactive optimization using defined procedures improves capacity utilization and availability.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Optimization effectiveness is quantitatively measured by utilization rates and cost-per-transaction metrics.
rubric.optimizing: Continuous automated optimization uses AI-driven resource allocation and workload management.

items.text: How well does the organization balance cost and performance in capacity decisions?

rubric.initial: Cost and performance are not balanced; either over-provisioned or under-resourced.
rubric.managed: Basic cost awareness exists but trade-off analysis is informal.
rubric.defined: Structured cost-performance analysis with defined decision criteria guides capacity investment decisions.
rubric.quantitatively-managed: Cost-performance optimization uses quantitative models with marginal analysis and ROI tracking.
rubric.optimizing: Dynamic cost-performance optimization continuously adjusts resource allocation for optimal value.
